## Plyboo

In late nineteenth-century London there were over thirty manufacturers making furniture from imported bamboo. Following a theme of her earlier designs using recycled plastics, Atfield lets the materials of Plyboo give character to the object, contrasting the random natural characteristics of the bamboo with the processed, standardized, manufactured look of the birch plywood. Steel rods are used to reinforce the bamboo at critical points. The result is durable

![](images/6a4bbd93f71682155f6439dfeb400b24f79aec92aa10b309d08dd1279e3ce3b2.jpg)

![](images/2d42abf80da6210c269dbb0869c65ad23a4ba2d8e8f72aba43947e5c5056f46e.jpg)

seating that may well revive the fortunes of bamboo as a material for the new millennium, casting off the unwanted associations between bamboo furniture and colonial verandas, conservatories and swimming pools. Bamboo is here again. It is modern, renewable, recyclable and toxin-free and is a ferocious producer of biomass in the right climate.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Jane Atfield, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>304</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Prototype, Float UpVP, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>316</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Bamboo, plywood, steel</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>295,339</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>2</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Renewable materials</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr></table>

![](images/0cb4e5f4858476363a77a56ce14271c95c53111b771b71ba764e73bee49a3b46.jpg)

## Ply Chair

Avoiding excessive usage or wastage of materials should be a guiding   
principle of any design   
in the twenty-first century The Ply Chair is the   
latest answer to the   
'Superleggera',   
demonstrating restraint, grace, economy, strength and character.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Jasper Morrison, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>309</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/2c3cad3681e853f3b1daa278146d273da5eee033ce96c8ae85ae7eaba2c5d335.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Vitra AG, Germany</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>326</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/2c383e1d9165a5c7d6f9daa1dcb8696667ea78967f506a0f8a376f0f1f921960.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Aeronautical-qualityplywood</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>339</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/6f08bbc5413fc5ef0e84d801cbb7a34200be885f18d7ebca88eddeff8baa93b3.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Economy ofmaterialsusage</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr></table>

![](images/a896b8c46f60c28b46c29f2a89b08e4377721570734bb8ed98d9e758a200f03b.jpg)

## Deck Chair

The structural properties of recycled plastic sheet have been thoroughly exploited in this combined chair and ottoman, which can also be used as a deck chair. This material is highly malleable when heated, enabling complex bending to produce results similar to pre-compressed wood.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Colin Reedy, USA</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>309</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>春</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Meta Morf, Inc., USA</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>320</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Recycled plastic, steel</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>283,295</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>•Recycled and recyclablematerials</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>a</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>IDRA award, 1996</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>332</td></tr></table>

## Daybed

This company has been manufacturing furniture, using special techniques for weaving twisted paper, since the beginning of the twentieth century. This daybed combines a contemporary shape with a traditional material by making the most of the manufacturer's extensive experience with this medium.

![](images/5a246ccc88c91cfd9dc24899627c41369d165fe95df07ce7ce29be369a457ab5.jpg)

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Nigel Coates, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>305</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/6cd8e6b43ff8879e256e97cefa88faa247e4f6c22618c537d0e21ed0d374b468.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Limited batchproduction by LloydLoom of Spalding, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>319</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Twisted paperand steelwire</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>288,295</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Renewable andrecyclable materials</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr></table>

![](images/3ef6e54570a6e212f4d255b4f538acd46927104cf194a8aed199116a88815349.jpg)

![](images/971dddb9e0f7f826511fa3fa9e171ddf13086cf9421927ae72ff66e16642436f.jpg)

## Earth Chair®

This simple sphere of stitched denim is despatched from the manufacturer empty and filled by the buyer with once-used plastic shopping bags or similar pliable plastic fill. The designermakers claim that it requires gkg (2olb) of such fill. Perhaps the item would be 'greener' if, instead of plastic bags, the filling were made of renewable or compostable materials.

## Diva (n) Paradox

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Earth Chair, USA</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>315</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Earth Chair, USA</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>315</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Denim</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>290</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>·Low-energyfabricationincludes self-assembly</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>328</td></tr></table>

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Pil Bredahl and LiselotteRisell, Denmark</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>304</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>One-off</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Rushes, polyurethane</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>276,341</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Encouraging use ofhistoric renewablematerial</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr></table>

Rushes harvested from freshwater reed-beds are inserted and set into a simple frame containing polyurethane foam. Organic materials of natural and synthetic origin are juxtaposed in a visually arresting form. Both materials are quite durable and when no longer serviceable could be incinerated or shredded for reuse as fill material.

![](images/0a3978bb44cb8f6381f1521ef27c77ab9ca684649c634e48a04cc2f4a881669a.jpg)

## Spring

Modular components can be used to assemble a range of furniture from chairs to chaises longues, fashioned from wood, wool and leather. Natural compounds such as waterbased paints and oils and waxes provide protection for the wooden parts, while the leather originates from a tannery that uses vegetable-based products. Easy assembly and disassembly facilitate repair and extend the life of the products.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>MO</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Galleri Stolen AB,Sweden</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>317</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Galleri Stolen AB,Sweden</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>317</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>timber, wool, leather,naturalfinishes</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>276,327</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Clean productionModular designfacilitating repair, reuseandlongevity</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Q</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Design Sense awards,Shortlist, 1999</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>332</td></tr></table>

##

![](images/39107e5d61c33128e2f7852eb1635da7bc83bae320a3cb41dba8adaf8fafb6dd.jpg)

![](images/51efd4056e7a69082a4458de6a2b23ae714d73d5256cf4a9650f464732ac85df.jpg)  
Moore explores the sculptural possibilities of recyclates, in this case HDPE sheet, treating them as new materials ripe for experimentation. The result is an eyecatching take on an old theme and an economy of materials use.

## Pouffe

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Isabell Moore, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>309</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/448bddc2a39e34ccc6e4ea6fcfb7514568ef78e41ae4718212ce8324c327aa57.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>One-off</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/ee18a091a38812e29e34d73ac529940d3f36311c7d9e3c2e6b6eb034ae7eaa57.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>HDPE recyclate, mapleplywood</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>339,341</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/a600c4f9060182650bc3b1afdba834d5d13269c2b01b93ce5c8323e643247327.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>•Recycled and recyclablematerials</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/fc06782d1eb814b405d27592150274af5ff2ec367f5a64ff24fd4e6ec6b33ade.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>IDRAaward, 1996</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>332</td></tr></table>

![](images/6d19a6f3ad774ac08460343813767e7d3f56b6f16c20ca5d136910550457ba21.jpg)

## 100-piece-kit armchair

Durable, richly patterned, native British yew wood offcuts and salvaged pieces have been reworked into a series of interlocking blocks, which, once assembled, form an armchair with considerable presence. Dolphin Wilding breathes new life into waste that would have been burnt or despatched to landfill.

![](images/d9fc8711ba088fe499156aa91ac9a96026dccd34527708c02adb4dbe00607685.jpg)

![](images/7a278beb5a97f29dd4e084bd939a290c76482853d4416dfea439426a34fc1764.jpg)

![](images/03f4dee3954535783d93aa55be29ea91b6ada954dfdb2f4dc941f61c39d37662.jpg)

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Julienne Dolphin-Wilding, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>306</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>One-off/smallbatchproduction</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Yew wood</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>339</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Recycled andrenewable material• Durability</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr></table>

![](images/cb598c79bf8de4dfd07a054e2c412dd9837d8ec3254bc9bb428e86f2fab20340.jpg)

![](images/abe1308847d3283c3c887ec31ac34835743c62e64c9a14b8379c237ba9a49542.jpg)

## Eco

These stackable chairs are cut from a single piece of veneer-faced ply and follow in the Scandinavian tradition of working with bent ply, as were the designs of Gerald Summers for the firm of Makers of Simple Furniture based in London in the late 193os. Simplicity, economy and functionality meet in this award-winning design.

![](images/29b004fe6441d40cee5e550c09ae090b695333a3125130badd3e9b1912530cf1.jpg)

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Peter Karpf, Sweden</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>308</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>春</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Inreningsform/iform,Sweden</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>318</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/8e52184d786c0535decad16b4d98910896982275edf4be7bf9363d76e4be4aac.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Plywood</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>339</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>.Renewable materials• Economy ofmaterialsusage and low-energyproduction</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327,328</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>4</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Winner of the iF EcologyDesign Award, 2000</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>332</td></tr></table>

![](images/e4a6687e3d067de73008796225f5d206edb294d2ae6001e8b6600331dda65764.jpg)

## Seating

## Eraser Chair

The innards of most chairs are hidden from view but Culpepper has chosen to celebrate the inner secrets of the Eraser Chair, which raises the value of recycled materials by bringing them to the attention of the viewer. Laid bare is the structural fabric of this design, which consists of 95 per cent-recycled wood felt attached to  rigid frame.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Michael Culpepper, USA</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>305</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>春</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>One-off</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Recycled wood felt</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>339</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Recycled andrecyclablematerials</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>IDRAaward, 1995</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>332</td></tr></table>

![](images/185adf7aa91def2358cedaea1ff4069ec72d3cc416aadefde0af2f2351019aa8.jpg)

![](images/a45282cdb24884a04f26ad409baa53d7abd6d474615fd327e0c79508726707a9.jpg)

## Eric

Cardboard is combined with laminated and painted plywood to produce an armchair requiring low-energy input to manufacture. Wettstein emphasizes the corrugated construction of the cardboard and joins a distinguished list of designers such as Gehry and the Campana brothers, who also make the most of the strength of this laminated material.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Robert A Wettstein,Switzerland</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>311</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>0</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>One-off/smalf batchproduction</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/58190df6ed61e54c0d1421d9c1f6550c0135a2ea01120442eab12fdb1f13ba1c.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Cardboard, plywood</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>339</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Renewable materials</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr></table>

![](images/a97f66fa35707a66f94d7ddc8753c4cebb9622658eab316a5ed015b40f5bf9ab.jpg)

![](images/810fe77f3c5ef223cbb5cfff6081f83f90ce072711071f6f0d56e11725375ca6.jpg)

![](images/8eba885d5e1f4fcfbc8c3faf65d7b9f1280bee9ea070873f85d0078f27f6d9b2.jpg)

## q-bac

A striking and comfortable easy chair has been created using ready-made components, such as aluminium ladder sections and rubber, with minor modifications. Existing manufacturing plant and capacity are utilized more efficiently to introduce new products. At the end of their lifespan, the components can be easily disassembled for recycling or reuse.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Gabriele Ackon, DavidZyne Productions, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>315</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>David Zyne Productions,UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>315</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Aluminium ready-mades, rubber</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>283,327</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>=Use ofready-madecomponents</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr></table>

## Ragchair

Waste rags and pieces of cloth are bound over a wooden frame using steel bands, mimicking the process of binding bales of recycled textiles, to create a comfortable yet unique easy or lounge chair. Remy has observed the textile industry recycling its products and neatly translated the idea into eclectic furniture that boldly states its origins.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>0</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Tejo Remy, DroogDesign, Netherlands</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>306,309</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>DMD, Netherlands</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>375</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Textiles, steel</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>290-1,295</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>.Recycled materials·Low-energymanufacturing</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327,328</td></tr></table>

![](images/d8ba1bb1abdc16751b06ad1c74829c9d8c7e24607a81b2750a89faec3c610d42.jpg)

![](images/6135b5f52451262300558902fa9c2614bc4184811c7fd1ecc8fb19f3dd1ac100.jpg)

## Schair

Following his experimentation in the 1980s with one-offs using salvaged materials, Tom Dixon designed this elegant cantilever chair. A steel frame is wrapped with woven rushes, creating a sculptural form. At the end of the chair's life materials are easily separated for recycling (steel) or composting (rushes).

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Tom Dixon, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>306</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Cappellini SpA, Italy</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>314</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/973c31ffd9d1bdb0f15d6b6d12f7d955c4d9ab020773699833fde29736941caf.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Steel, rushes</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>276,295</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>·Economy ofmaterialsusageRenewable andrecyclable materials</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr></table>

## RCP2

In 1992 Atfield created the RCP2 chair with a simple but robust construction technique using a radical material challenging the ecological awareness of designers and the public alike. New techniques for recycling HDPE waste created a new sheetlike material whose constituents readily divulged their origins. The rawness of the early work of designers such as Atfield and Bär and Knell contrasts with the more sophisticated use of recycled sheet HDPE in Bopp-Leuchten's lamps in the late 1990s. Further innovation in manufacturing using recycled plastics can be expected over the next decade as attempts are made to close the plastic recycling loop.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Jane Atfield, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>304</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/6f69bf1788bf11271e43c8b7965f49c47fec51c7e43e4022d7cfa2591f681345.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>One-offs and small batchproduction</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/315c9581ec9b3cd420fbb5decf7c14bacb58190467467be4d50fd018300529d1.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>High-densitypolyethylene (HDPE)</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>347</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>.Recycled and recyclablematerials</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr></table>

![](images/eb63b5fe8cc653282c2b8efca7ff48313260d070b2f933f633ca61096bbdec75.jpg)

![](images/f593834bc6c595cabab52602a7a8718839661e6f95d384fcfa407c2599ddf1c1.jpg)

## Seating

## Trinidad No.3298

'Industrial craft' production will undoubtedly prosper in the twenty-first century if the workmanship and graphical form of this ash chair are a measure of the output of today's furniture manufacturers.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Nanna Ditzel, Denmark</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>306</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Fredericia FurnitureA/S, Denmark</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>316</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ash wood, metal</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>295,339</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>•Renewable material</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr></table>

![](images/addb331875fd5ac5e087b4749e0d09e333a21d9ccc2bdd38a63954d99eb970a8.jpg)

![](images/9ae5467a5b677cc45f87fd53dea81495e1eb6e2b4b57e8f922f3928b5eea66a6.jpg)

## Flying Carpet

A steel frame with supporting rods suspends stiffened felt in mid-air to create a seat that sways as the user moves around. Economy of materials use combines with ease of separation into pure-grade waste streams for recycling the materials at the end of the product's life. This imaginative design explores new applications of wool felt to furniture.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ilkka Suppanen, Finland</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>310</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>8</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Cappellini SpA, Italy</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>314</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Steel, felt</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>290,295</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Economy ofmaterialsusage. Renewable andrecyclable materials</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr></table>

## Ghost

Purity of form and function can often be achieved by focusing on the exclusive properties of one particular material. Cini Boeri and Tomu Katayanagi have taken a single piece of 12mm-thick (c. 1/2-inch) toughened glass and cut and moulded it into an extraordinary object. They juxtapose the contradictory characteristics of the material – its fragility and toughness – and create a durable, rather timeless design. Ghost provides food for thought on how other familiar materials can be modified or mutated to fit new forms and functions. Being composed of a single material facilitates recycling at the end of the product's life and encourages closedloop recycling, where the manufacturer uses its own recycled materials to produce new goods.

<table><tr><td></td><td>Cini Boeriand Tomu Katayanagi, Italy and Japan</td><td>305</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>Fiam Italia SpA, Italy</td><td>316</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>Glass</td><td>295</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>Recyclable single material - Durability</td><td>327</td></tr></table>

![](images/bebdd3ad2c3286a8b9aab84e695053f35c9f362d6e97f92370d6cedc2bcede56.jpg)

![](images/b158cbd1045da646e64b1906fdb7848a00f3be0d0232cc3a84845426e4383293.jpg)

## Chair and ottoman

Slabs of heavy-grade industrial felt, typically used for noise insulation in military vehicles, are bolted together to create an archetypal armchair. An ottoman emerges from the offcuts. The original felt slabs are transformed from the utilitarian to the purposeful, yet retain their honesty of origin. Like the Danish designer Niels Hvass, who has made a

similar chair from used newspapers, Atfield reminds us to keep it simple and create zero waste.

<table><tr><td></td><td>Jane Atfield, UK</td><td>304</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>One-offs</td><td></td></tr><tr><td></td><td>Felt, steel</td><td>290, 295</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>·Economy ofmaterials usage • Zero waste production</td><td>327, 328</td></tr></table>

![](images/1aa1071ae0429bdf36f13a363268a6cbdb111153b5de97c8f7da21515c9f4dd2.jpg)

IKEA a.i.r./MUJIAIR sofas

IKEA are peering into the future and testing the way forward for sustainable product design. This example by Jan Dranger sets the stage for manufacturing furniture using recyclable plastics and interchangeable covers, as wear and tear or fashion decrees. As resource scarcity bites in the twenty-first century, manufacturers will have not only to use recyclable materials but also to develop business models that ensure that product take-back keeps materials in a closed recycling loop.

## Bench

<table><tr><td></td><td>Jan Dranger, Dranger Design AB, Sweden</td><td>306</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>News Design DfE AB for IKEA, Sweden</td><td>321</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>Plastics, nylon or polyester, cotton</td><td>283, 291</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>•Recyclable materials •Economy ofmaterials usage • Low-energy transport andassembly</td><td>327, 328</td></tr></table>

In a imaginative turnaround, paper from trees is recycled in a 10o per centwaste-paper material called 'Shetkaboard' to become a substitute for sawn timber in this indoor/outdoor bench. Why cut down more trees when waste paper will do the job nicely?

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>StanleyJ Shetka, USA</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>310</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>One-off</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Shetkaboard</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>280</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Recycled, renewablematerials</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>a</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>IDRAaward, 1995</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>332</td></tr></table>

![](images/31ea32ca5632f874ffa92244c38d61a16b44ec006f6adec7b4390fc797aa387e.jpg)

![](images/417c4078770d045a09ae62c743c2e8715fb865cd8b325e2cdb7f06ee9ebecdb4.jpg)

## Chair

Discarded industrial pallets are the raw material for this chair. After machining, the individual wooden lathes are held together using steel bolts. The chair is easily disassembled for repair or recycling.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>James Varney, USA</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>311</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>One-off</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>R</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Recycled pallet wood</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>339</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>.Recycled and recyclablematerials</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>a</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>IDRA award, 1998</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>332</td></tr></table>

This knock-down chair uses lightweight Gridcore, made of 100 per cent-recycled paper honeycomb sandwiched between laminated paper surfaces, for the sides and back. Solid cherry wood is used for the arms and rear leg. The furnishing fabric comes from DesignTex, a US company known for reducing the environmental impact of its textile range.

## Kids furniture

The confetti-like colours and patterns of the recycled plastics in these furniture items are tailor-made for creating a stimulating learning environment for the young child. Demonstrating this material's versatility for making robust, fun furniture, Reedy introduces some nice touches such as the hand holds for carrying the chair and rounded edges for safety.

![](images/544829407c4ad8b9c924c782ac70f27e5e00d77c7466c176064bc824d1caff39.jpg)

## Knotted chair

A loose, flexible, macramé form of aramide fibre braid is dipped into a solution of epoxy resin. The resin is hardened by drying at high temperatures to produce the necessary rigidity for the purpose. This remarkable chair is a blend of imagination and technology, which fits the Factor 4 philosophy of 'doing more with less'. However, a few reservations surround the technosphere materials. Epoxy resins need careful handling during production and the resultant aramide reinforced with epoxy resin is a composite that could prove difficult to recycle.

![](images/cfc8cd7dd8cd9a4462b367cee10fca711850233191d6b59496636ccefc57f6a2.jpg)

## Krogh chair

The single-piece back support and arms are made of pre-compressed wood. Many types of wood will respond to precompression treatment. The process involves mollifying the fibres with steam and then compressing them, which causes the fibres to adopt an accordion-like zig-zag structure. This results in an overall shortening of the wood by 2o per cent, which reduces to 5 per cent when the compressing force is

removed. The wood is malleable at this stage and can be bent before being allowed to dry. Once dry it maintains its original strength and flexibility.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Erik Krogh Design,Denmark</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>306</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/78c6037a9ffa74b607b944ad463fd6f3f6ac93e5c9458081f08efb774ee9bcda.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>One-off</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Pre-compressed wood</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>339</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Renewable materialwithimprovedcharacteristics</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr></table>

![](images/45ebb68980c4f5f349455f9f4f90ccb0176c0e684e9610018875b2c3f250575c.jpg)

## Little Beaver

Part of Gehry's 'Experimental Edges limited edition for Vitra, the Little Beaver comprises offset layers of thick cardboard with large corrugations, glued and stood on end. Both the material and the construction technique reveal numerous permutations for working these materials and contrast with his earlier

furniture work with cardboard in the Wiggle series of 1972, now being produced again by Vitra.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>D</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Frank O Gehry, USA</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>306</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Vitra AG, Germany</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>326</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Cardboard, glue</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>287,299</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Renewable andcompostable materials</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327,330</td></tr></table>

The colourful, circular polypropylene seat clips over the powder-coated steel frame to provide a versatile, stackable stool or footrest. It is economical in its use of materials and stylistic language.

![](images/7e13658f6ab4611d7164616beb2a41602f8b4adf8f2e633c65117f631e12d661.jpg)

## Stool

Simplicity is the key driver in this design, resulting in economical use of materials and low-energy input during fabrication. Durable materials ensure robustness and a long life. Mahogany can be salvaged, obtained from sustainably managed forest (as certified by the FSC) or substituted by a sustainable temperate hardwood.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>1</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>ijs designers (BridgetReading and Moniquevan den Hurk), UKandNetherlands</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>307</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Limited batchproduction</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Stainless steel,mahogany</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>295,339</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>-Economy ofmaterialsusageLow-energymanufacturing</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327,328</td></tr></table>

## Stül, SE 68, 1998

An economical 1950s design, which originally used plywood seating and back rest, has been reproduced using multicoloured plastic sheeting originating from waste packaging. Where renewable materials are in short supply or costly, recycled plastic offers a viable alternative.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Professor E Eiermann,Germany</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Wilde &amp; Spieth GmbH,Germany</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>326</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>HDPE sheeting, steel</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>295,341</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Recycled andrecyclablematerials</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr></table>

![](images/6b910d3752c2e7050e4106b8278aa22ed3adc27dc755ea85420d4d2b78c0ea69.jpg)

![](images/352b4923a9a82d71235d70736cd489c2d8a50ce186203a25f582d011e11bfd6a.jpg)

hollow arms and 'angleiron' legs. Starck brings his usual wit and economy of line to this chair, which is equally happy in a garden, an urban loft or a café. As it is fabricated entirely from PP with a small, easily removable, stainless-steel plug around the drainage hole in the seat, it easy to recycle the materials at the end of the item's life. By 2030 manufacturers may even be requesting that their products be returned by the current custodian for dismantling and recycling of components and materials. The material content of the Toy Chair will then be valued as much as the comfort and pleasure given through its lifetime.

<table><tr><td></td><td>Philippe Starck, France</td><td>310</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>Driade SpA, Italy</td><td>315</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>Polypropylene</td><td>341</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>•Recyclable single material Economy ofmaterials usagein manufacturing</td><td>327</td></tr></table>

## Thinking Man's Chair

Tubular and flat steel are combined in a deliberately 'engineered' look, further enhanced by the red oxide type finish complete with written dimensions. A durable design for indoor or outdoor use, which, being made from a single material, is easily recycled.

## The Draught

Wicker is imaginatively combined with steel for this exciting range of chairs. Traditional weaving of wicker is abandoned in favour of methods in which the withies are held tightly together with steel or inserted into solid beech. The results bring nature, with all its innate variability, into the living space.

## XL1 kit chair

This lightweight chair, weighing just 2.2 kg (4.7 Ib), won the 1999 Jerwood Applied Arts Prize coordinated by the Crafts Council, UK. Marriott combines materials and

## Wiggle series

Originally designed in 1972 as economical furniture and manufactured by Jack Brocan in the USA, the Wiggle side chair has been reproduced by Vitra from 1992. Each layer of corrugated cardboard is placed at an angle to the next layer to provide significantly increased durability compared with the folding cardboard chairs by the likes of Peter Raacke and Peter Murdoch in the 1960s.

ready-made components, which are readily available from local builders' merchants and DIY stockists, into an honest, economical, multipurpose chair. In the UK, DIY interior decoration, building and gardening are obsessions, so it is refreshing to see a designer encouraging such enthusiasts to apply themselves to designing furniture.

## Chair

Dolphin-Wilding preserves the quirks of nature's patterns in her unique wooden chairs, letting the natural forms dictate the structure of her handcrafted reincarnations. In doing so she takes us back to days before 'craft' work became a highly skilled profession or before 'industrial design' produced technologically refined furniture.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Julienne Dolphin-Wilding</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>306</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>One-off/smallbatchproduction</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/69688592552079d7a0765d2e922ab87c56933537104d3f3066597030d6ecb562.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Timber</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>339</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>.Renewable materials·Low-energyfabrication</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327,328</td></tr></table>

## Flower pot table

Clay flower pots are given a fresh, sophisticated image in this simple but wellexecuted design for a durable, functional occasional or side table, which uses raw materials that are readily available worldwide.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Jasper Morrison, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>309</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Cappellini SpA, Italy</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>314</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Clay, glass</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>295</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>·Abundant materialsfrom the lithosphere=Economy ofmaterialsusage</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327,340</td></tr></table>

## Ash round table

Combining excellent rigidity and ample legroom, the simplicity of this design relies on the strength of the solid ash, which comes from local English woodlands. A range of table sizes to seat three to ten people is manufactured to the same basic design. Trannon Furniture successfully blends traditional furnituremaking techniques with a modern aesthetic to produce durable, quality seating, tables and shelving.

## Mooving Image/Sony flat-screen coffee table

Old TVs never die, they just find a new home. Jam's TVs don't work but they still grab the attention. Electronics manufacturers are being forced to realign their entire business strategies in accordance with EU legislation (such as the WEEE Directive), which encourages them to take back their products. Fabricating furniture from TV tubes isn't really dealing with the problem of disposal (of heavy metals, gases, glass) but it provides an interim solution while suitable disposal methods are sought.

![](images/f2ef139407263cd125eb14a97163f973d8846981f55a6b27521459e4179ea5e7.jpg)

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Jam Design oCommunications, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>307</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/fb46f57c606eec6873943e9e934b6bc7fdab718eca78f3bb90b34fad03ccb033.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Jam Design oCommunications, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>307</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/7be35abca4ddea631fbd7f6239266b523edd3fc5b1af64b2c40b275c48edea6f.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>TVtubes, steel, castors</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>341</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/144a7a6c8898b0acab429c084f6771e8ee7406c16448e89e0ed574804182c381.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>·Reuse ofend-of-lifecomponents</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>330</td></tr></table>

## Concrete table

Concrete has a similar embodied energy to timber, or lower, so its use in creating robust, durable yet customizable furniture is welcome.

## Plaky

Sophisticated extrusion and moulding of the ABSpolycarbonate blend of plastic recyclate adds value to this waste stream by turning it into a desirable item of furniture. The anodized aluminium pedestal stem completes the 'up-market' effect.

## Saita

Almost 90 per cent of the steel in circulation has been recycled at some time, so it is refreshing to see steel being used with great sculptural panache in

this table design. Long the preserve of architectural and structural engineers, steel offers fresh   
perspectives for furniture designers.

![](images/14720215049736affd34d5e0e6078cb2f5608a6f15d510672f2fb7a4515df374.jpg)

![](images/30ec082e1eb4a70c92733badf81d4005d17564d730fac4592993aa536d1fd73f.jpg)

## Tables

## Schraag

This table-cum-desk minimizes on materials by using three legs, not the traditional four, for each lightweight aluminium trestle. A range of standard 2 x 0.9-metre (6ft 7in x 3ft) tops can be chosen from laminated bamboo, glass or red multiplex. Simplicity and ease of assembly bring the old-fashioned trestle table into the modern world.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Martin van Severen,Belgium</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>317</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Bulo, Belgium</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>313</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/7c2d27bb29d40cd14b8b7cdb98ec59baf970be000d10f8f4cc513a69c7bab474.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Aluminium withbamboo, glass ormultiplex</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>281,295</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>.Renewable materialoption- Recyclable materials·Designfordisassembly</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327,328</td></tr></table>

## Table0.85×1.20m

![](images/bb0b2a8c0bb4e682b8fbe0e36bb6b8b8904f90167ccf36df61de6ed98326ebd7.jpg)

Table design is stripped down to the bare essentials - a wooden worktop, four tubular metal legs, the whole easily disassembled. No fuss, minimal production costs, easy flat pack and lowenergy production input.

## Ledge

The density and strength of ABS, even in recycled form, contributes to lightness in design, in Christopher Connell's vision.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/dec685ddfc7f321e9b6dbe27ba30d927e625a33a9a4d6935820a65f31041ad5c.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Paul Nijland, graduate,Design AcademyEindhoven, Netherlands</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>309</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>a</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Prototype</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/7e6569955e48536f0527ded21a04f54964321b930c87d756214fc64ffe4771af.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Wood, metal</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>295,339</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>• Economy ofmaterialsusage·Low-energymanufacturing,distribution andassembly</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327,328</td></tr></table>

## Table

Hertz reveals the workability of Syndecrete® in his individualistic, sculptural tables.

![](images/e70b9bbbcc64e23c38d4aa366d2d387dfb41dfd721ec128fd8169dba4dd54ca4.jpg)

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>1</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>David Hertz, USA</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>307</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Syndesis, Inc., USA</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>324</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Syndecrete®, glass</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>295</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>• Recycled materials</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr></table>

## Side table

Syndecrete® is a lightweight composite concrete utilizing mixed industrial and postconsumer waste, pulverized fly ash and PP fibre waste. The material is wel suited to detailed moulding and can be polished to reveal a terrazzo-like surface.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>David Hertz, USA</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>307</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Syndesis, Inc., USA</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>324</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Syndecrete®</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>295</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Recycled materials</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr></table>

![](images/8e9affff35cad05f9a2172f3ee08ddb6c4af6c4d8cbd77567a5a61eb5d9bdbfc.jpg)

## Folding table

Easily mass-produced, with low energy input, this simple folding table combines low cost with transportability and the use of Environ and Ranger MDF particleboards.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ann Girand and BrianChampian, USA</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>307</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>One-off</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Environ, Ranger MDF</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>278</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>•Recycled andrenewable materials</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>o</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>IDRAaward, 1995</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>332</td></tr></table>

![](images/b412bff7e2001049ad7ff15e15516b626d0f3b64810c4d6284fa6d4001b55496.jpg)

## Table

Gridcore, a recycled paper honeycomb core sandwiched between layers of fibre sheeting and veneer, provides sufficient rigidity to support a glass table top.

![](images/611d5456c9ea5b0d8175b487128159971d44e1ce99f95d363a641a550c28cd42.jpg)

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/27d207221eea0624e3162d64411665bfdada3747579ffcf87f65db5ba2628201.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Dan Cramer, USA</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>305</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/c527c738a77eb0d21494f68224d28fb9ae6fb560188988c45b291fd1fe5612ef.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>One-off</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/ada6fa34d7a0bc4e2412f78bd9dde7dbfcc368ac7197e50797317c07dd48176a.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Gridcore, glass</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>295</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/ad490ac37ff6193754c43bf8f6f4139a13da78cc975639e32d9f36c3b77accda.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>= Recycled and recyclablematerials</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/0d4893457fe48bb50c58c7019b72042a0dc40a4b74e32cd6aa91bc44ad9d8090.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>IDRAaward, 1998</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>332</td></tr></table>

![](images/14bfc362cf9d2f61f93b8cf75233888025d01daa31504d0fc7afe84ddc833e5d.jpg)

## Table Op-Lá

Morrison's economy of design is exemplified in his ability to make the connection between the usually separate roles of displaying and serving food and drinks. This dual-function tray-cumtable uses the bare minimum of materials.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Jasper Morrison, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>309</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/820a9f022bc3006f6a9bf5b638e254e5624b1b6df11c08a420babb8f8d7ff3c8.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Cappellini SpA, Italy</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>314</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/85da4c6b054ef5322994bbf92b7315086006be647495e6b4d76d61507da09877.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>ABS, stainless steel</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>295.341</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/995acaafae37e1adb2bb9f3b19061fc3c2d23c85bd7ccbbba76154e2c84710a1.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Dual-functionobject</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>329</td></tr></table>

## Nested tables

Jurinec sees cardboard as a noble material with great worth in its unrefined naturalness, low cost and transient durability. Cardboard encourages minimalism in design and is non-materialistic. It is also easily returned to the recycling loop.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>KsenkjaJurinec, Croatia</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>307</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>One-offs</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/bfa9e7b3b1e088039673d8715d7d266dddb30fe28f4678b40b18a6b33478ff94.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Recycledcardboard</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>288-9</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/23762fdf1aa2fb724c298cad4b1ae912232eba7d8b9fcf013f73efbad0bcd4f9.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>•Recycled materials</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/425d8176e3b2c3d30bd12bfbf2a6e354d374d19c6dec271a1b3e03888167ba59.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>IDRA award, 1997</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>332</td></tr></table>

![](images/772bd5c3371c11cd20f332a4782faba86c23f5a166746c1612c828bb426662a7.jpg)  
This concertina-like bookcase has an interesting juxtaposition of natural materials. The craft aesthetic has always embraced experimentation with nature's primary materials but further possibilities are emerging to create a new 'industrial craft' production.

## Bookcase

## Chest ofdrawers

An assortment of salvaged drawers is reincarnated as a new chest of drawers in Remy's functional yet quirky design. The drawers looks as if they could have been strapped on to the back of a pick-up truck and indeed the designer encourages this metaphor by binding the drawers together with the webbing and steel ratchet used by truckers. Chest of Drawers is an object suspended in time, neither permanent nor transient, and demonstrates the recycling of dozens of original (drawer) designs that fashion had consigned to the junk shop.

<table><tr><td></td><td>Tejo Remy, Droog Design, Netherlands</td><td>309</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>One-off/smallbatch production, Droog Design, Netherlands</td><td>307</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>Old drawers, webbing, steel</td><td>341</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>Reused and ready- made components Cold, low-energy construction</td><td>327, 328</td></tr></table>

![](images/b721672a41ccdc1e5c6c78424d9ac3eaeb14eedc0fce88eb9eba7fe94576a4e7.jpg)

<table><tr><td></td><td>Jan Konings and Jurgen Bey, Droog Design, Netherlands</td><td>308</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>DMD/Droog Design, Netherlands</td><td>307, 315</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>Maplewood, paper, linen</td><td>288-91, 339</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>Innovative use of natural, renewable materials</td><td>327</td></tr></table>

## Shelving and Storage

## I Just Moved In

The layers of meaning are as numerous as the shelves in this intriguing design, which promotes individualism and juxtaposes the old, the new and the banal. The design raises a question about the attitudes of designers: should the construction details of Wiesendanger's bookshelf be published so that anyone can assemble a bookshelf on similar principles but using locally found materials? Or is the limited batch production an exclusive process that guarantees status to the purchasers?

![](images/9fde066929301aa4f5316cb206437eb9c0045c60164fcd3e74680ef8b238a7a6.jpg)

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Köbi Wiesendanger,Switzerland</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>311</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Limited batchproduction, Avant deDormir, Italy</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>313</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Pinewood, cups andsaucers (used to prop upshelfunits)</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>339,341</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Reuse ofready-madecomponents• Renewable materials</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr></table>

## Boox

Plastics are the by-products of our desire to burn fossil fuels and are integral to our modern lives. Yet plastics are perceived as a major culprit in despoiling our

environment, even though they have made possible resilient, functional and technologically advanced products for over half a century. Philippe Starck's 'Boox' shouts, 'look at me, I'm plastic, proud of it, cherish me, I'm a valuable resource.'This modular extruded and injectionmoulded shelving system is well conceived and should persist for many decades before it is disassembled ready for a new lease of life as another recycled product. Boox gives plastic credibility and in doing so makes us reevaluate the essential worth of the material.

![](images/d40dd1b64f105ed2caa501b3eb511233bde72de4feac99db21a89bd29c96287a.jpg)

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Philippe Starck, France</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>310</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Kartell, Italy</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>318</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Thermoplasticpolymer</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>282</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>.Durable modulardesign.Recyclable materials</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr></table>

## Shelving and Storage

## Plug It

Boredom is a factor that consigns many an object to the auction house, secondhand dealer or, in the worst scenario, a landfill site. Roberto Feo's modular shelving system ensures that the user can rearrange and add new modules to refresh and restimulate the visual senses at the same time as extending the storage volume.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>0</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Roberto Feo, Spain</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>306</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>春</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>El Ultimo Grito, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>306</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/f6f20ed77ced1e52a034eabaa703459ead9d22e9e5821256e711356bfd970470.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>MDF</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>292</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/9fa3f4e8f8d9a33596faf05be9a4c6c589bc4c18f262c23d5434b275f550b09b.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Modularmultifunctional design-Upgradable</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>329</td></tr></table>

![](images/3e451e87537e0f2cc05dcb17ce54ec1c3498e1213b2e94bf57d13645dbe47433.jpg)

![](images/8b562a68bb1e8c79d47e886f5b0074dfff34aa23171a9254e36412cbd89f3534.jpg)

## Modular bookshelf

This dual-function bookshelf and/or storage unit is pared down to an essential modular component of a box frame and removable box 'drawer'. It is a simple, versatile system that can be expanded to meet the user's needs. It offers excellent storage capacity in relation to its footprint and can also double as a screen to divide a room.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>0</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Giulio Polvara, Italy</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>309</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>春</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Kartell, Italy</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>318</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/3a4e402dcb442f26ad2a5de453862d30782c8b2e2fa5060a5ab697e0ad07d738.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>ABS, lacqueredpolyurethane</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>347</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Modular storagesystem</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>329</td></tr></table>

## Robostacker and Hola Hoop

Traditionally manufacturers strive to serve specific markets and, where possible, increase their market share. Jam are a design group focused on developing symbiotic relationships with manufacturers to explore whether their ready-made components can be adapted to create fresh products for new markets. The 'Robostacker', produced in 1997 in collaboration with Italian white-goods manufacturers Whirlpool, converts three stainless-steel washingmachine drums into a

storage unit. Developing the concept further, Jam created the Hola Hoop shelving using painted or stainless-steel-finished drums and glass sheets. Ready-made components can be obtained from pre-consumer (factory) or post-consumer (municipal or other waste-disposal centres) sources. Jam have liaised with other well-known manufacturers to produce flat-screen mobile coffee tables from components made by Sony. Reuse of manufactured components seems to offer endless

![](images/b8b051996f74ea0d4c350b96c2559c4633b0acd1b43bba32a823dbf4bcf1764c.jpg)

![](images/026ec6216b7f95e264218badb0107977cab946b24affd967d572d873199bf2dd.jpg)  
possibilities but does not absolve the designer from examining the green credentials of the original components and manufacturers'environmental commitments.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Jam Design αCommunications Ltd,UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>307</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Jam with Whirlpool,UK/Italy</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>307</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Stainless steel</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>295</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Reuse orre-applicationofready-mademanufacturedcomponents</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr></table>

![](images/c63fee34afbc107d70f18e2469db53d151ed656de03a64f9a36a6287c394ea0e.jpg)

## Es

Nine beechwood rods are inserted into four plywood panels and locked into place using plastic rings. Grcic tests the boundaries of stability with a design that wobbles yet doesn't fall over. His design appears to fly in the face of man's desire to remove nature from the process of manufacturing, being deliberately made to look naïve and in a DIY style. The rods permit the shelving to double as a coat rack and clothes stand.

<table><tr><td></td><td>Konstantin Grcic, Germany</td><td>307</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>Moormann Möbel, Germany</td><td>320</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>Beechwood, plywood, plastic</td><td>283, 339</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>Renewable materials, economically applied · Ease ofassembly/ disassembly</td><td>327, 328</td></tr></table>

![](images/d24e4c6d8dcfa81ca5b2f6e4a1a67bbed5af3ca983ee96e93fb7a3e700daf372.jpg)

# Shelving and Storage

## Sten

The Sten system of storage and shelving is a series of standard flat-pack components, which can be bolted together in a range of bespoke combinations. Shelving, uprights and storage-box panels are manufactured from untreated Scandinavian softwood from managed forests or IKEA's own woodlands. Coach screws and ties are used to fix the individual elements into a rigid structure and the assembled unit can be easily disassembled for reuse or recycling. The shelving is robust and capable of holding up to 500kg (1,100lb).

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>0</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>IKEA, Sweden</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>319</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>IKEA, Sweden</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>319</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Wood, steel</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>295,339</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>.Renewable andrecyclable materials·Low-energymanufacturingandassembly.Customizablefurniture</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327-9</td></tr></table>

![](images/360ccf9f0b93afbdf51a61abd94b3e45253eb8c5406f91afe076ade33a612585.jpg)

![](images/f3032a4b4ef2cce922dbe571e195658fb14b51fecebe40123dd05d606b231738.jpg)

## Shell

Moulded aircraft-grade plywood, just 3mm (about 1/8 inch) thick, is fixed with 3-D metal corner fixings to create a basic shell that can be fitted out internally as required with shelving and a clothes rail. A longitudinal hinge permits much better access to the contents than conventional wardrobes.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>C</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ubald Klug, France</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>308</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>春</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Rothlisberger,Switzerland</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>323</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/accc9398454b8870e8bec40a08d905e3001038731654aabc7b30e8d61d765fcb.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Plywood, metal</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>295,339</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>·Economy ofmaterialsusage·Low-energymanufacturing</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327,328</td></tr></table>

![](images/e5e1e2ba2efe4f1c612f0836c8756943b751b89b0a82167155d726d00778160b.jpg)

![](images/6312b1c34130accdce1d85adc809d137a76db40a49880129b8d77542abf35435.jpg)

![](images/27940c3a5d4a0e0e8870a175dd216df0d016de1018463ee95fe0d87c564097c0.jpg)

## Hoover

This lightweight wardrobe features stretchable side panels, which provide extra capacity for those awkwardly shaped objects. N2's minimalist philosophy excludes extraneous detail and incorporates a reductionist approach to the use of materials.

<table><tr><td></td><td>Jörg Boner, N2, Switzerland</td><td>305</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>sdb industries, Netherlands</td><td>323</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>Various</td><td></td></tr><tr><td></td><td>Economical use of materials Greatercapacity than traditional designs</td><td>327, 328</td></tr></table>

## Ecotable/sideboard

Joinery skills are applied to this table/sideboard, which is made of 95 per cent Environ, a biocomposite, and 5 per cent Durawood, an HDPE plastic lumber recyclate. These materials complement the simplicity of the design elements and echo the style of classic Shaker furniture made from American hardwoods.

![](images/6949ac0eb93ddc1c4d2416fa8ae93301921f921726d1f00656cb3edf19633820.jpg)

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>George Ettenheim,Ettenheim Design, USA</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>306</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>春</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ettehneim Design, USA</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>306</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/f50781597ce4bdb7009c7a2f0a6cedffe7d38c6692a45ed020c4cb3205e4d1e8.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Environ, Durawood</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>278,293</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Recycled materials</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Q</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>IDRA award, 1995</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>332</td></tr></table>

![](images/2468f4f86ef8254d7e85f982976a7d93ce485734b8be89cfcd771f6d91121e73.jpg)

## Italic

There are just two components in this beautifully simple shelving system: bent steel rods whose ends are dipped in natural liquid gum to provide a good grip, and wooden shelves. Users

could add extra components to expand their existing shelving.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Lorenz Wiegand,Germany</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>311</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>春</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Prototype</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Steel, liquid gum, wood</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>287,295</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>=Economy ofmaterialsusage·Low-energy production</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327,328</td></tr></table>

![](images/babd1382c5e9ca6450b51c9587b3af4628feef4fec0969533c9f889cd55c35c6.jpg)

## Shelving and Storage

## Console and shelving system

Bär + Knell demonstrate the versatility of recycled HDPE plastic board with this eclectic range of furniture. The message is unequivocal: waste is valuable and recycled waste extends the palette of materials for the designer.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Bär+ Knell, Germany</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>304</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>One-offs, Bär+ Knell,Germany</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>304</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Recycled plastic</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>2</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>•Recycled content</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr></table>

![](images/a6e291178e898a88a98d1914fe6dd973ebabf069f87428527846f684dd0bf8ff.jpg)

![](images/ff0ac7fa5da71654ced41587acbeefdfa657be8424844daa648d19ed0f48e93c.jpg)

![](images/574960b5fe9b7db583c10bf572adf6b8a7692393b259ebf6afb5f92e0c0ef453.jpg)

![](images/2702f9e563757a9561fd3cf5fddf2514a8268178556c2787352442b2f6f492a2.jpg)

## Turris

Components include shelving and side panels of Fiberbond Wallboard made from recycled newspaper and gypsum and rolled steel angles using 6o per cent-recycled steel. The wheels that allow the shelves to be moved are made from used rubber conveyor-belts.

![](images/f8fc611d330a6ba862182816a1301629ae83f401a59862f21064abedf3656194.jpg)

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Sheri Shumacher, USA</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>310</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Small batch production</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Recycled steel, FiberbondWallboard, reusedrubber</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>283,295</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Recycledandreusedmaterials· Easy assembly/disassembly</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327,328</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>4</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>IDRAaward, 1996</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>332</td></tr></table>

## Shelf system

Lightweight materials commonly used for packaging can provide a viable alternative to 'traditional' shelving materials such as steel, aluminium and wood. These shelves are constructed using techniques similar to those used to manufacture moulded packaging for eggs and electronic goods Part of the challenge to designers is to reappraise conventional practices and test the suitability of materials, especially biocomposites, for new applications.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Jasper Startup, StartupDesign, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>310</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Small batch production</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Recycled newspapers,reused fruit trays</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>341</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>•Recycled materials.Reusedcomponents·Compostable</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Q</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>IDRA award, 1995</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>332</td></tr></table>

![](images/c366a290f8fb5e6ad2c81f6ee6add310aacae9d5d3a8956ee69957779f93665c.jpg)

## A Collection of Mammoth Pillows

Anthropometrics fixes the scale of human objects in the home, but if you break the rules refreshing new concepts can emerge. The humble pillow scaled up to 'mammoth'size becomes foldable sofa, an armchair for a corner between walls, a mattress or a comfortable zone on which to relax.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Judith Kant, graduatestudent 2000, DesignAcademy Eindhoven,Netherlands</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>307</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Prototype</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Furnishingfabric, filler</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>285</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Multifunctionalfurniture</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>329</td></tr></table>

![](images/529a185abf15482d6e084ab5b8256861ff493a2e0cdcfe5716355933617d58e8.jpg)

![](images/429c35aa25df5ee38e43cdcffb982545642b4a98aabe0073cfc52afafe95877a.jpg)

## Multifunctional

## Table-into-chestof-drawers

With a simple action this sparse chest of drawers cantilevers gracefully out to form an occasional table. For the Azumis this design reflects Japanese cultural needs where rooms are often multifunctional and futons and tables are removed or reconfigured as required.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Shin and TomokoAzumi</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>304</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>#</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Small batch production,Shin and TomokoAzumi</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>304</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Wood, metal</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>295,339</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>· Dual-function design</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>329</td></tr></table>

![](images/ad3be6d4fef5e1c107fbd7ef46cfca5a027f397ffe40fe71a096f61cadae9e2f.jpg)

## Communications furniture

The time is right for a design revolution in the computer industry. Although the iMac revitalized the styling of computers, they still remain working machines designed, primarily, for the formality of the office environment. Sander de Klerk's characterful stool, reminiscent of a Barbara

Hepworth sculpture, is a fun, portable, wireless computer with a touch screen linked by an umbilical cord to the stool. The user can take this charming object with him/her to the bathroom, kitchen or garden. In the twenty-first century we should expect to see less of the grey personalities of existing PCs and more machines that are really useful even if they aren't even switched on! Here's a step in the right direction.

![](images/b1f806c31c6871e9940af828a7e022fc2af8730cc6ae442b02c5911daafe3c2b.jpg)

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Sander de Klerk,graduate student 2000,Design AcademyEindhoven, Netherlands</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>305</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>a</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Prototype</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Various</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Improvedfunctionalityforcomputerhardware·Designforneed</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>328,329</td></tr></table>

## Dia

Adaptability and durability are the two primary prerequisites for furniture that is intended to survive the elements and robust use in the garden. This range offers a high degree of flexibility - the chair has an upright and a low position, the table height is adjustable and the sunbed has eight possible profile permutations. Polished stainless steel and strong fabric, impregnated with waterproofing and UVstabilized, ensure a long life. Thanks to these highquality materials, this range of furniture is also suitable for indoor use and thus offers flexibility and dual-functionality.

![](images/c7265e3d08a4402d4656d61d8c7925e00d88c67045e62b0fcb4554fdc4d2ecc9.jpg)

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Gioia Meller Marcovicz,UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>308</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>ClassiCon, Germany</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>314</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Stainless steel,waterproofed/UV.stabilized fabric</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>290-1,295</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>• Durability,multifunctionality</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>329</td></tr></table>

![](images/b1c2f5119c364420c35ce0c4766b30aa63271560a23e6b2523547ef766917c7e.jpg)

## Multifunctional

## Flexipal

These identical interlocking plastic modules can be articulated and held in fixed positions by tightening the adjusting screws to configure a range of furniture from tables to chairs, beds and platforms as desired. This encourages the owner to experiment with his or her own concepts and offers flexible functionality.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>J</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>R Miles, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>308</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Retail Place Ltd, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>323</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Plastic</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>283,295</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>-Multifunctionality,ease ofupgrading•Single material tofacilitate recycling</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327,329</td></tr></table>

## Kaststoel

The joined halves of this interesting seat/shelf/ magazine rack amply demonstrate the multiplicity of uses to which a merged object can be put.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Paulo Nervo, graduatestudent 2000, DesignAcademy Eindhoven,Netherlands</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>309</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>0</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Prototype</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Polymer</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>282,296-7</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Multifunctionalfurniture</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>329</td></tr></table>

## Kokon

Old wooden furniture   
is revived by covering   
it with a PVC-based   
coating. The   
opportunities to create quirky new custom   
furniture are legion but the technique needs   
further refinement to find a substitute for PVC,   
whose environmental   
track record is poor.   
How to isolate the   
timber of the reclaimed furniture from intimate contact with the PVC   
and what to do with the items at the end of their lives are unanswered   
questions.

![](images/3ed06fbfc7dc240808e46dd54045cfb0a55316a79664c83307f3559a007bfbec.jpg)

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Jurgen Bey, DroogDesign, Netherlands</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>304</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>a</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Limited batchproduction, DroogDesign, Netherlands</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>306</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Reclaimed furniture,PVCcoating</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>341</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Reuse ofready-madecomponents</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr></table>

![](images/13f6642b2cef6980deb52e407d45a6bdb7d84fdbac4dc40df3137e304ece8eb7.jpg)

## Nature Technology Symbiosis

As civil engineers use   
plants to reinforce   
geotechnical structures   
(bioengineering), designers can use plants to   
strengthen their designs.   
Edwin Wannet believes that the symbiosis of   
technological and living   
elements in this 'living   
cabinet' raises questions   
about the types of products that could be developed,   
product-life expectations   
and emotional responses.

![](images/f51f24858c177c2840c5b40ec62c1d64ffbe67e6ddb2c15dbd9a407f38d11345.jpg)

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Edwin Wannet,graduate student,Design AcademyEindhoven, Netherlands</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>311</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>中</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Prototype</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Wood, glass, livingplants</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>295,339</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Multifunctionality Living, respiring object</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>329</td></tr></table>

## Low Living

Reviving maritime techniques and skills, such as spool knitting, creates strong, rhythmic textures in this multifunctional range of Low Living products suitable as blankets, wraps or cushions. Turn down the heating and retreat to your cocoon!

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Gonnie Constansia,graduate student 2000,Design AcademyEindhoven, Netherlands</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>305</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>春</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Prototypes</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Wool</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>290-1</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>-Renewable materials</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr></table>

![](images/7ba6f92fa49df9fcedd31267c4e653dfaabe19069db5c76bc994ced38a7a3bab.jpg)

## Pouffe

A temporary bed, suitable for day-time lounging or use by an overnight guest, unfurls out of a pouffe, the exterior upholstery doubling as a blanket.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>N2, Switzerland</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>309</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Prototype, sdbindustries, Netherlands</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>323</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/290641a0fe61b7eb69d477423b5e104fe5217f3fb88c2ffe698d1c300b54da4c.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Aluminium, birchplywood, polypropylene</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>295,341</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Multifunctionalfurniture</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>329</td></tr></table>

## Hide-away

This intriguing design invites the user to take out a book from the shelf, crank the handle to raise the day-bed and settle down for a good read.

## Wave

Two layers of felt sandwich layer of polyurethane to create a flexible matting, which is curved and stiffened at one end by changing the mix of elastomers in the PU. When not in use the mat can be wound around the formed end. The result is a unique object that can be used as a mat, a recliner or a chaise longue.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>C</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Martin Hoogendijk,Netherlands</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>307</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>DMD/Droog Design,Netherlands</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>306,315</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Felt, polyurethane</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>290-1341</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>·Multifunctionalobject</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>329</td></tr></table>

![](images/f4c5187488c0c2c5247259d9760c61cf2e92037af6c81ff9cafada5e94a1caa8.jpg)

![](images/82e251aefaa265e2e89c65721dffb65d0dd4380bc9016a7bef2b8301a3345473.jpg)

## Table lamp and table chair

Dual-function design is   
pared down to its ultimate simplicity in Richard   
Hutten's witty yet practical furniture. He demonstrates the same thoughtful   
economy, bordering on the austere, in the Table Lamp and Table Chair, pragmatic and lovable designs   
which will be   
cherished.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Richard Hutten,Netherlands</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>307</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Limited batchproduction, REEEL,Netherlands</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>323</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Beechwood, MDF,metal</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>295,339</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>•Dual-function designs.Economy ofmaterialsusage</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327,329</td></tr></table>

![](images/8b2005db48aa96abf30713dd48921998ba48f9271c28a5dd15dfc6d66db547da.jpg)

## Mind the Gap

For those who like to relax with a coffee and a magazine, 'Mind the Gap' is the only accessory required. It combines efficient dual functionality with economical use of materials and imbues the combination with a cheeky persona. There's really no need to buy a table and a magazine rack ever again.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>El Ultimo Grito, Spain</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>306</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>春</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>EL Ultimo Grito, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>306</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Rubber, steel/alloy</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>283,295</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>MultifunctionalityEconomy ofmaterialsusage</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327,329</td></tr></table>

![](images/fc478a183c75edb68b98c28443fd30dbe11e688c49e18213ef1af1df72f1c652.jpg)

## Nomad

For those who like nothing better than to lounge around on a deep pile carpet here's the ideal solution that avoids the use of all those unwanted big cushions while giving your back a rest. Wanders has metaphorically and literally elevated the status of the humble carpet well above the tacky examples of shagpile of the 1970s.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Marcel Wanders,Netherlands</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>311</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Cappellini SpA, Italy</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>314</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Wool, metal</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>290-1,295</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Multifunctionalobject</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>329</td></tr></table>

![](images/cc823909e3e46473f624447278924865b94d0d3b421f7cf98f847d05f6650113.jpg)

![](images/4411db3c4d5fa6f88626980e15c8f9288717402bad553266961088bd5667cbe6.jpg)

## Wire-frame reversible bench

Inspired by the   
technological achievements of supermarket shopping baskets and 3D computer modelling, the Azumis   
were able to create this   
lightweight bench, which   
can be reversed to make a chaise longue or stacked   
with other benches to   
provide shelving.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Shin and TomokoAzumi, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>304</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>春</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Small batch production,Shinand TomokoAzumi, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>304</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Nickel-plated orpowder-coated steel</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>295</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Multifunctional design</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>329</td></tr></table>

## Anna Chaise

Ratchets on the alloy frame permit the sides of this armchair to be lowered to suit the occupant and convert the armchair to a chaise longue or day-bed. An excellent example of practical, multifunctional furniture.

<table><tr><td>UK</td><td>Gioia Meller Marcovicz,</td><td>308</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>Habitat, UK</td><td>317</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>Alloy, padding, fabric</td><td>290-1, 295</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>Multifunctionality Inexpensive materials</td><td>329</td></tr></table>
