## Miscellaneous

## Three and One-Half Minutes

This lightweight screen encapsulates fragments of time in old processed film stock bound in an aluminium frame. Although the aluminium is easily recycled at the end of the product's life, a question mark hangs over the film stock impregnated with silver halides and other chemicals. Will future processes be

## Carta

Shigeru Ban creates structures with cardboard and succeeds in elevating this humble material to a new aesthetic level. His use of cardboard tubes in projects as diverse as furniture, temporary housing for refugees and buildings for communities reveals superb understanding of the capabilities of the material.

devised to dissolve and separate the constituents? In the meantime the screen represents an ingenious way of storing the non-recyclable component.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Jam Design &amp;Communications, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>307</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Jam Design oCommunications, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>307</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Aluminium, old filmstock</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>295,341</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>• Recycled materials</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr></table>

![](images/0c69ad16ad9f7c8f0a702dafebe1cdd4da8a703bcaac222980851fa3002cb3a1.jpg)

![](images/f63b57096ec4e5addb56111cfb1d6287de3d789078412226347e76f55f407979.jpg)

Carta is a range of furniture that makes minimal use of lowembodied-energy materials.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Shigeru Ban, Japan</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>304</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>春</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Cappellini SpA, Italy</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>314</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/c216c9df10018507ec224d93aafd07010d870d1bf76528bfa59058477b696cbc.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Cardboard</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>288-9</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/cfd9e233d45f3370a1b3005e695de1bfe0a31b4af5dc152506997e90704110bf.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Renewable materials.Economy ofmaterialsusageRecyclable</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr></table>

![](images/f5dd272717111f410a0b8157c403c31e5b8cf1dc2cbd3b6311d57c9417f1319b.jpg)

## Fasal corner joint

Fasal is a new material consisting of wood fibres, cornmeal and natural resins, which can be readily moulded or sprayed. It provides an alternative to non-renewable materials such as plastic polymers. Jacqueline Andringa has

![](images/92036fc10e3ccfccc3761066d1b3a170ae7cd3237030fc610e593d8bd9dffb00.jpg)  
extended its usefulness by creating a universal corner joint that locks any two or more equal-sized components, opening up opportunities in furniture and product manufacturing and timber-frame construction.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>0</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Jacqueline Andringa,graduate student,Design AcademyEindhoven, Netherlands</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>304</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/57c8d7b6d41959ce8aef890aec16b260ff24e63d2f8817319a005b5fd6ca6582.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Prototype</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/3986cd49c37a1ec0fb49504da55f84a341fba214573a3115789624dce399bf10.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Fasal</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>278</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/0a52265208decdc6574163869864d5ef294e550dd20db358e8ffc7743bba1da5.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Renewable andcompostable materialUniversaljointingsystem</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327,329</td></tr></table>

![](images/0ec80c703668d26cca146425353214adea054c37674ef742025ad49edd37498e.jpg)

## Frontal

Transparent plastic cushions are stuffed with discarded sweet and food wrappings, offering a graphic reminder that 'We are what we eat'!

## Herz

Steel reinforcing rods,   
similar to those used in   
construction with concrete, are welded and bolted into a simple frame to which a moulded leather   
breastplate is attached,   
providing a functional,   
minimalist coat stand. The materials used are easily recycled and the design is both aesthetically pleasing and durable.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Anthologie Quartett,Germany</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>304</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>0</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Robert A Wettstein,Germany</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>326</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/c8351212eb1d1a0c2fe42f0e776ac8a8fdaa8d5007799c07b6cc3dea27b58d2e.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Leather, steel</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>285,295</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Recyclable andcompostable materialsEconomy ofmaterialsusage•Low-energymanufacturing</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327-9</td></tr></table>

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Bär+ Knell, Germany</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>304</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/abaf5f25a63d722d33bbc471c79890d1f40cf7b428655a7e96946a8241bc6a4c.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>One-off</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/64dc8b1c8166f652623dbd3d10eaa989311e9d39fff5ac22c54ccf7c8ce63818.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Plastics, plastic-coatedpapers</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>283,295</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/27ee3a69a2654b4f445169805a74ca0929f9d63a52d96c8c841a28d55370a051.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>·Encouragesrecycling·Low-energymanufacturingvia&#x27;self-assembly&#x27;usinglocally sourcedmaterials</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327,329</td></tr></table>

![](images/1a8ad0f82b89bec0848f3cbd0550fdde608dc186192fb4f9a47c021c0c207fa1.jpg)

![](images/21814307e442ba4e7cb370a32a9f0bdd78f73d9b119841541e515389c2a0115f.jpg)

Hülsta'Muvado'range

Germany is undoubtedly one of the 'greenest' consumer markets in the European Union and Hülsta is a significant manufacturer of domestic and office furniture with a proven commitment to environmental performance. It was one of the first companies to register to the quality assurance standard, ISO 9001, and its entire production is certified under the Blue Angel eco-label scheme. In collaboration with Danzer, a leading veneer company, Hülsta initiated the 'veneer passport' guaranteeing that it does not originate from a tropical rainforest. Only four of their current ranges of furniture use solid wood, again not sourced from rainforests. Particleboard or MDF is the primary material. In-house designers apply lifecycle analysis to extend the projected lifespan of products, of which most are already expected to last between thirty and forty years.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Hülsta, Cermany</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>318</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/c1f29a538e115b5d03ab439037edb308b2b285896214a0fc94cf6d2dda17331b.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Hülsta, Germany</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>318</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/e94d825ed79503998c0448d64950e6f11c5f2b89f1bd7d5a56f9466bd0e14e3e.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Veneers, solid wood,particleboard</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>278-80</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/f85694454f12385f661e5d4c3f869dd4d35460eeacdf5ac22414bd702d7b6bc2.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Renewable materials• Blue Angel eco-labelCorporateenvironmental visionandpolicy</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr></table>

Mimicking an ear of corn, this lightweight coat rack, made of seven thin, wave-shaped, plywood cutouts attached to a metal-rod frame, is an ideal resting place for coats, hats, umbrellas, bags, newspapers and more throughout its entire length.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ubald Klug, France</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>308</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/2ee35c107c551aefe5607a59ac207d3469eda3660e5fac84abaa61e1686c5880.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Rothlisberger,Switzerland</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>323</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/922386aa18a91a17dab58050a8c69c62c38070c3e106583bb469accf0085928c.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Plywood, metal</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>295,339</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/e12f88f3af530081f2c3368ddbffbdfcaa7660e597e0fa5eef39f766d5878302.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Economy ofmaterialsusage-Multifunctional</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327,329</td></tr></table>

## Good Morning Miss Moneypenny

Not all waste newspapers and magazines need to be manhandled down to the nearest local authority recycling point. Save those special editions and insert them into a specially cut polypropylene tube to create an eclectic, customized hat and coat stand. El Ultimo Grito wittily create a valid green design by combining recycled and recyclable materials with self-assembly. They revitalize the 'Heath Robinson'approach to invention and elevate recycling to a new art form. Tired with the current look? Simply exchange the old papers with fresh, up-todate material.

![](images/3235e76f19f5ba763f051bbfb552840db77d048f902b02ada35a1f9dfe6f0d37.jpg)

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>El Ultimo Grito, Spain</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>306</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/3eeea4f7de9421defaad82e1e4b1960e4781ef07d3fb635d899dd05aeaa411ea.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>El Ultimo Grito, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>306</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/eaffccfe2a3328a787b910e6a410e8966ae7e384c0608083e4b067141c7c4afb.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Newsprint</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>341</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>.Reuse ofdomesticwasteLow-energyconstruction and self.assembly</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327,328</td></tr></table>

## Hut Ab

Aluminium fixings allow simple machined pieces of ash wood to articulate around a pivot to provide a multifunctional clothes and hat stand, drying rack or structure for suspending house plants. Low-energy requirements during production make this an efficient design at low cost.

![](images/524bc23c21ae7caeb0c62d16c167aeb48f108b33bab6558d67f8221cb7d39270.jpg)

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/4c08ecd17da38820404fcaa92bab2b9495797c357775f6425d51b272122cd730.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Konstantin Grcic,Germany</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>307</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Nils Holger Moormann,Germany</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>321</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/bf747174944b7332a7b0e76c3cb7cb42c292695e2ca5baac8569ccdff3061f81.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ashwood, aluminium</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>295,339</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/7ad724300cefb1f768c784fa43c6a90f2d38bf7e498787b567acc212a1e0d63e.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Multifunctional design.Renewable andrecyclable materials·Low-energymanufacturing</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327,329</td></tr></table>

## Screen

A slender metal frame supports a web of interwoven plastic string. This lightweight screen can be fabricated from virgin or recycled materials, doesn't require specialist machinery to construct and is easily dismantled at the end of its life, when the materials can be salvaged. Fabrication can easily be adapted to suit locally available materials.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>D</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Fernando and HumbertoCampana, Brazil</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>305</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>One-offlimited batchproduction</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Metal, plastic string</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>283,295</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Economy ofmaterialsusage</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr></table>

![](images/af27ffdf500334c8b398a09de34af7a10ff78d3ae886f0856435a77bf22af1fd.jpg)

## Miscellaneous

![](images/bb867da0a75b213bbcd822d841a33b97f67d10e0a8866b8680e8f9944d73b5c9.jpg)

## Screens, various furniture

Designer-maker Jason   
Griffith goes by the   
venerable title of   
'underwoodsman'.   
The ancient craft of the   
underwoodsman can be   
traced back five thousand years in Europe. Using a   
wide variety of timber taken from native or naturalized coppiced trees (those   
deliberately cut to cause   
multistems), he fashions everything from hazel   
hurdles or screens to   
tables, benches and chairs. The output of the   
underwoodsman is   
sustainable thanks to lowenergy production   
techniques based on

manual labour and locally sourced wood as well as encouragement of the system of coppicing, which helps maintain biodiversity.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Jason Griffiths, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>307</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>One-offs and smallbatchproduction</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Coppiced wood of oak,ash, yew, sycamore,lime, alder, hazel, birch,willow, sweet chestnut</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>339,341</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/06316515603a16028172d473fdd970836971bfaf9ba06937e18aaf556cf2b79a.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>.Renewableresources•Encouragesconservation andbiodiversity·Low-energy production</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327,328,330</td></tr></table>

## Portable double bed

Anyone who has moved house knows that the most cumbersome item is the double bed. Not so for this superb example, an entire double bed tha't can be neatly carried in its own suitcase. Bucking the trend for selfassembly furniture to be flat, stylistically drab and infuriatingly difficult to

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Peter Steinmann andHerbert Schmid,Switzerland</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>310</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Atelier Alinea,Switzerland</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>312</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/ff4466589368829194e021bc8d95ee721baa97a37614f24d67cfcb91b2f95e99.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Beechwood, plywood,steel, rubber</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>283,339</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>• Portable, self-assemblyfurniture. Economy ofmaterialsusage</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327.328</td></tr></table>

![](images/4a0b3442b4e2bf89675a5150c734a2593043afd915318182c1c05f88cfe443cc.jpg)  
assemble, Steinmann and Schmid have devised a construction system  
that is not only rapid to assemble but also visually appealing.

![](images/a14c9f1a80d0aa068e69a363e1ccc1b8313bef101036654c9d631897f314e67d.jpg)

## Bernini

Fabricated from Tyvek, a HDPE fabric containing 25 per cent-recycled content, this flouncy chandelier illustrates the drapability of plastic fabrics. Water-based adhesives and the use of a single material facilitate later recycling.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ruth McDermott andRina Bernabei, Australia</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>304,308</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>One-off</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Tyvek</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>301</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>•Partrecyclatefabric-Recyclable</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>a</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>IDRA award, 1996</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>332</td></tr></table>

## Coral, Polyp, Spores

Moerel demonstrates the flexibility and hence the beauty of her raw material in this series of designs, which are inspired by the same basic module, a ceramic sphere. Coral and Polyp are two variants for unusual pendant lights, while Spores are individual candleholders. Economy of scale is possible if largescale production can utilize different modular spheres to make a range of products.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Marre Moerel,Netherlands/USA</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>308</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Small batch production</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Clay</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>295</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>2</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Use ofabundantmaterials from thegeosphere·Durable</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>329,340</td></tr></table>

![](images/7f016df35d86f56ab3a4effee00e7de4eb3a192a8f4b70d038e4be10aecb48d3.jpg)

## Hanging

## Quentin

The designers were inspired by the complex forms of folded cardboard packaging and utilitarian products such as egg boxes. Working in collaboration with a local Glaswegian manufacturer, they sought to create a product utilizing pulp from recycled newsprint and paper-mil waste. An individual shade comprises two identical but mirrorimage halves, which are formed in a mould where the pulp is vacuumdrawn. These innovative lampshades are semiopaque, giving a unique

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>lan CardnuffandHamid van Koten, VK &amp;C Partnership, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>317</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Universal PulpPackaging, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>325</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Newsprint, paperwaste</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>341</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>2</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>• Recycledandrecyclablematerials·Low-energymanufacturing</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327,328</td></tr></table>

![](images/a9ed111a5985f445c943e57c2d8d0f0087aa28d164b19546dcb9637e29d7e4be.jpg)

![](images/954c49483d4e161c7e51b75645ade413447edba767a908a49ee50d271e8a6aff.jpg)  
light output. The shades can be deployed individually or in groups to form customized arrangements. At the end of its useful life the lampshade can be repulped ready for its next

reincarnation. Potentially this product can sit within a closed recycling loop, ensuring maximum reuse of paper fibre and minimizing the energy required to remanufacture the product.

## Fish-lamp

Drilling holes in a standard pendant lamp fitting enables the brass suspension arms to support the frosted glass pane, which acts as a diffuser for the downlighting spotlight.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>O</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Mikala Naur, Denmark</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>309</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Limitedbatchproduction</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Brass, glass</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>295</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>.Recyclable materials-Economy ofmaterialsusage</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr></table>

## Flirt

Flirt is a redesigned object using a plastic float made for commercial fishing nets to create a dramatic lampshade with two different lighting zones - a diffuse zone from the body and a white cone from beneath the shade. An aluminium 'hat' is the only specially made part.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Sergio Macchioni, Italy</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>308</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Prototype</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Fishingfloats,aluminium</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>295,341</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>•Use of ready-madesLow-energymanufacturing</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327,328</td></tr></table>

## Lampshade

Interlocking moulded forms of corn-starch biopolymer fit together to make this organic shaped lampshade. This prototype avoids the use of adhesives, is recyclable and/or compostable and demonstrates the potential of biopolymers to replace non-renewable plastics.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/cf3d2761acd139c00fb979d13f1ff0f28452d0802e0aba3fb50f706cca3c2526.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Brian Dougherty, USA</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>306</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/2a474a7f1fb7ff47bd498bd57371c60d6a8d57645ea6e8ea9589d49bdab99671.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Prototype</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/9a4fbcf18327e44d1b21139d33d7e3641fd3c5989f9461982571ffbd67c97114.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Corn-starchbiopolymer</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>282</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/550341a2387bd57ccc9b5fd1e2ee8621f82309d39a10db33639cdbc7456a1395.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>-Renewable,compostablebiopolymer</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>330</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/571629dcf88f217a59587190c37fded1871dadd8cec5ec95a62710cafd0e2ebc.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>IDRA award, 1998</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>332</td></tr></table>

## Lampshade

This reversible shade permitschoice of two strong lighting directions depending upon whether the reflector is uppermost (for down-lighting) or on the underside (for uplighting). This eloquent design embodies principles of minimalism and dualfunctionality, both of which are very relevant to designs with reduced environmental loads.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/2abb1c2d54c82afd4ab2ddf0ec577011a996ba1e08dc2be7442620e43b06d922.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Sebastian Bergne, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>304</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/3f2d6a2b44391813a0f4949624e4cd8a4e94f5d7b9eced180ddacb0284fc0000.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Radius GmbH,Germany</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>322</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/671bb36b5d5cd43909434fb3c76fc283a1f52d27a3966d7c54bad1a92e4cb4d3.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Steel</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>295</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/191be8904c21adb97856f6e5b3113ed3bda59785b20d171b884acde1413428b2.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Dual-function design·Economyofmaterialsusage</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327,328</td></tr></table>

## Miss Ceiling light

Efficient use of a single natural material creates a lampshade with sculptural characteristics, permitting shafts of light and a warm glow to penetrate the semiopaque natural plywood and creating a dramatic light source.

## Milk-bottle light

Since the early 1990s designers have responded to the challenge to consider their ethical responsibilities to the environment. In the Netherlands Tejo Remy explored the issue using discarded plastic milk bottles and in the UK Jane Atfield did the same with her RCP2 chairs using recycled plastic sheeting. As a consequence the message - that modern design must use recycled materials – is eloquently delivered.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Tejo Remy, DroogDesign, Netherlands</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>306,309</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Droog Design/DMD,Netherlands</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>306,315</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Discarded bottles</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>341</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Reuse ofwaste ready-mades</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr></table>

![](images/aa3da3eff4704d325b4810e28ff265a51528fdad6b9b7b5be544d778e943c862.jpg)

## Northern Fleet chandelier

Shards of broken glass are painstakingly assembled into a cascade of light in this unique chandelier. The jagged edges of the glass make an exciting contrast with the sheer beauty of the final form and the design gently mocks at the cutglass chandeliers of grand houses and public buildings. Quality, one-off designs may enjoy long lives, since they may attract greater custodial care than run-of-the-mill, massproduced objects.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Deborah Thomas, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>310</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>One-off</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Glass, wire</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>295</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>• Recycled materials</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr></table>

## Loop

Distributed as a flat-pack pendant shade or floor light, the Loop, made of polypropylene, simply snaps together.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Roy Sant, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>310</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Roy Sant, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>310</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Polypropylene</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>341</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>2</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>• Lightweight, recyclablesingle material</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>329</td></tr></table>

![](images/0d57852b9238978fd55ec52832c38f26a051684eeafd2bfaeda1fe83f7095f79.jpg)

## Bogdan light

Wherever there is a power point let there be light! With the 'sucker' feet on the housing of this minimalist portable light you can attach it to any

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>10</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Erik Espmark, Sweden</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>306</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>O</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Prototype</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Plastics, copperwire,brass</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>283,295</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>·Multifunctionality</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>329</td></tr></table>

smooth surface. Except for the bulb holder, all other parts are readily available from existing manufacturers.

## Cape light

The lightweight polypropylene structure is assembled by pushing together the steel studs. A swivelling head permits direct down-lighting for home-office working and up-lighting for those romantic domestic encounters.

<table><tr><td></td><td>Stephen Philips, UK</td><td>309</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>Stephen Philips Design, UK</td><td>309</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>Polypropylene, steel</td><td>295, 341</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>·Dual-functionallamp Lightweight materials .Low-embodiedenergy oftransport</td><td>328, 329</td></tr></table>

## 'ComeBack' series

Plastic packaging waste is reincarnated as a beautiful series of shades for table, standard and pendant lamps. The diversity of colour of the original waste source is reflected in the random, mosaic-like

arrangement in the manufactured sheeting that is the base material for the shades.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Bär+ Knell, Germany</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>304</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>0</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Bopp Leuchten CmbH,Germany</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>313</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>HDPEwaste</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>341</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Recycled material</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr></table>

## Floor, Table and Miscellaneous

![](images/ad0b7377b36d7f95da2fd26a3fe7cf74a1b7b66585034131591a881bbf28228c.jpg)

## Flamp

This wooden-based table lamp is dipped in phosphorescent coating so that it absorbs the energy from sunlight and reradiates it for up to twenty minutes. An ideal 'emergency' light after sunset.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Martí Cuixé, Spain</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>307</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Small batch production</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/5f88ca53596076b6dd86353969a729577e064d08c5818137fb757d863f50ad0d.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Phosphorescent paint,wood</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>286-7,298</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Solar-powerednon-electric light</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>329</td></tr></table>

![](images/aa13d9dc56caf1dd2f695776feee5b7d06fd5a61edb1542ce544f919f452a432.jpg)

## Clips

A simple stainless-steel frame clips over a discarded drinks can and supports a polypropylene shade. As your favourite brand of drink changes you can dispose of the old one (at a can bank) and insert a can that held the flavour of the month.

<table><tr><td></td><td>Bernard Vuarnesson, France</td><td>311</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>Sculptures-Jeux, France</td><td>323</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>Polypropylene, stainless steel</td><td>295, 341</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>·Encourages reuse of ready-mades - Reduction in materials usage</td><td>327</td></tr></table>

![](images/6f682553803548075c505d4ac78c63307eb7639cb7c73159efe891bfd10d6df8.jpg)

## Corkscrew/lamp

Complex messages originate from this seemly simple design by Car! Clerkin. The candle set in the wine bottle is given a sophisticated turn as ready-made components are clipped together, giving us a new look at the phenomenon of electricity. This design can encompass the whole social spectrum, from wine buffs keen to impress with their empty vintage bottles to cash-poor students.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Carl Clerkin, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>305</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Small batch production</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Reused wine bottle,corkscrew, electricalcomponents</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>341</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Reused and ready-made components• Easily disassembled</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327,328</td></tr></table>

![](images/77d715d274a90b1a683dc3bd10ddf5d398882cef4688b4481fb0e3814dfd8dcf.jpg)  
Hand (to Hold)  
This lamp generates surreal mood lighting by combining a familiar readymade, a rubber glove, with a 4W lamp unit.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Anette Hermann,Denmark</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>307</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Frandsen Lyskilde,Denmark</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>317</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>日</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Latex, lamp</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>283,341</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ready-madecomponent. Renewable material</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr></table>

## Table/floorlights

Recycled cardboard may carry a print message from its previous incarnation as packaging. Reuse of cardboard in a new context, in this case a lamp, extends the life of this otherwise transient raw material, which is just waiting to be recycled. In short, this lamp is the temporary custodian of the cardboard, which has its own life to be lived over and over again.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>KsenkjaJurinec, CrupaDizajnera, Croatia</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>307</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Prototype</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Recycled cardboard</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>341</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>•Recycled materials</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>9</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>IDRA award, 1997</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>332</td></tr></table>

![](images/a37e9a79ce0da06517faf9fcf446f2edbb466fbff0e80de7de1a5edf8af2d24a.jpg)

A room divider made up of plastic floppy-disk cases on a low-voltage (12V) wiring loom provides a unique display for a range of semitransparent pictures and casts a pattern of shadows and light.

## Lumalight lamp

This family of free-standing lights, made of recycled paper, reveals that the use of recycled materials does not inhibit creativity. In the hands of the right designer, the fact that the materials are obviously recycled is lost in the beauty of the overall design.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Roland Simmons, USA</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>310</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>6</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Interfold, USA</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>318</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/aa6a5edee0683c6aff429de5a6bc12584396c4ad97c0089e9cec904bb1f80d37.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Recycled paper</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>341</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/66293f2a01aed652153cefd07950fa20060ea33957172190822585a05ea5a45c.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Recycled materials</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>中</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>IDRAaward, 1995</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>332</td></tr></table>

## e-light

The e-light integrates a number of technological improvements over conventional desk lamps. The lifetime of the lighting filament is twenty times greater than that of an incandescent bulb and two to three times that of a fluorescent bulb and uses one-fifteenth as much mercury as the latter. Creating a light spectrum similar to daylight, it is five times brighter than a tungsten bulb. As the e-light produces negligible thermal emissions, the need for heat-resistant materials is significantly reduced. Components can be easily separated, facilitating recycling and reuse. Reversible joints and compact design provide flexible lighting configurations and a small footprint.

![](images/f9007173fa136eedb62111a4d58c34623d6495eb1106e298c45066c57f973f16.jpg)

![](images/f9f48acde1f13a7a2d4f7a36c7be53b4cf87f986364e0cca1b845089aba99f71.jpg)

## Obinjo

Candles and paraffin lamps were the portable light sources usurped by the invention of electricity and the tungsten bulb. Yet electricity tethered lamps to a fixed circuit and output sockets. So it is refreshing to see that Frits Vink has reintroduced us to the benefits of portable lamps. Obinjo recharges itself on a base connected to the mains supply and is therefore completely portable. With this we can wander from room to room without having to switch on any fixed lights.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Frits Vink, Netherlands</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>317</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/a7db7b6c8a7b6a1d20959a60f901083a02e2286709adebabd5f27efc50bf1443.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Prototype</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/bcd5751a76037160502efd2dcf872af29c99691df9d21e1186b29311b44461f7.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Various</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/c1f98b6524fed98dad999f547d7db4c8f2c3f1f9797e558a414fc2fdb69b2f32.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>.Portable, rechargeable,multifunctionallighting</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>329</td></tr></table>

![](images/27f5e268827c0b96fc67565fb0f0626502aef7deab6dd259cc2ec3282c3b7723.jpg)

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/d890722fe58cdc719e9c56685bfb5fa1596c9c52255a0d1b354669e6c28c5718.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Paul Topen, Designed toa&#x27;t&#x27;, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>306,311</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/83c0959e0ffd8d7f9a716b53a27a7e3ddbcd4402a805f6400cd3eec5a6608247.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Designed to a &#x27;t&#x27;, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>306</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/099ea54f1d62a81e98a2f10b89b3dada5832bc0d9e96b128fa330f6cdb882355.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Steel, plastic, electricalwiring</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>347</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/7ea5d894878192c81c532c0c5cbf7c5906878786af13ba3c3d21edc33d3bb860.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>.Reuse ofready-madecomponents</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr></table>

## Tube

The familiar fluorescent light gets the minimalist treatment from Christian Deuber. A slender synthetic tube protects the light with steel- and rubber-footed closures, allowing the light to be placed wherever it is required. The use of fluorescent bulbs, in this case 58W, which are much more efficient users of energy than incandescent sources, adds to the versatility of this product.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Christian Deuber, N2,Switzerland</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>306,309</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Palluco, Italy</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>321</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/dfdd3680ffeb54ef4bf31da136aacc16c93537c71a189130f5dc3a3cc407b029.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Fluorescent light, steel,rubber</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>283,347</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/31ae517b1a22a18c1fb4e13c62f2fff06900600ddfb96ccb99281a6e267042c3.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>-Multifunctionallight• Economy ofmaterialsusageLowenergyconsumption</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327,328</td></tr></table>

## Mini desklamp

Good ideas are often recycled, but the Mini Desk Lamp actually goes one step further by reusing part of an iconic design of the late 1950s, British Leyland's Austin Mini car designed by Aled Issigonis. Original Austin Mini parts for the distinctive sidelights are rehoused in a plastic body, fitted with an automatic on/off tilt switch and painted in the original body paintwork colours. The rebirth of a mini Mini classic?

![](images/49740b901cc800a3cbe48fc85128150c934d53f3d9b72d80c694f4aa7f2246fd.jpg)

![](images/d289edd791192743c68714a4648d2d6136234b30d05401944f35d501c810e86c.jpg)

## PO9810/10C,11/11C, 12/12C

## Pharos floor lamp

<table><tr><td></td><td>Jasper Startup, Startup Design, UK</td><td>310</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>Startup Design, UK</td><td>310</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>Bamboo, papyrus paper</td><td>281</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>.Renewable materials ·Low-energy manufacturing</td><td>327, 328</td></tr></table>

The designer has succeeded in transforming garden cane with a cylindrical papyrus shade into an elegant, minimalist standard lamp. Natural variability within the papyrus paper creates a range of unique textures and light patterns, mimicking the spun-fibre shades of the 1950s.

In a clever extrapolation of scale, the table lamp becomes a floor or standard lamp. These lightweight constructions combine visual stimulation and humour in an economical design.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Marcel Wanders,Netherlands</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>311</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Cappellini SpA, Italy</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>314</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Wire, polymer</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>296-7</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Economy ofmaterialsusage</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr></table>

## PO/9902C-D

Framing the bulb not only focuses the viewer on the light source but also provides a protective package during distribution and retailing.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Jeffrey Bermett, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>304</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>春</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Cappellini SpA, Italy</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>314</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/43062f29b9aec41cd5b38fe14895db78897b54a873502ae92cbefefe59f30af4.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Cardboard, lampholder</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>288-9,341</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/550eebe7b95a2c2d547ffb8de0d3eae1120483061e33338c9f172facf2d3d93a.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>• Economy ofmaterialsusage</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr></table>

## Soft Box

Special clays are fashioned into durable lighting units. Ceramics are traditionally used for bases for table lamps but here the material forms the base and the shade.

## Floor, Table and Miscellaneous

![](images/76519cd9ac213540a102c1de344cd3592bb4f6d59c65a58fea1f9523d6b595ee.jpg)

## Post It lamp

A strong cardboard tube with plastic end caps arrives through the post, the contents are extracted and within minutes it is assembled into a compact but functional table light. While not particularly robust, the Post It Lamp minimizes transport emissions and costs, as the packaging is used to form the lamp base. This is a neat idea that can be extended to other design applications.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>2pm Limited, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>312</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>春</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>2pm Limited, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>312</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Cardboard, plastic,paper, electricalcomponents</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>341</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Economy ofmaterialsusageReductionin energy oftransport and assembly</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327.328</td></tr></table>

## Tablelamps

Inexpensive extruded tubes of 100 per cent-recycled HDPE are cleverly juxtaposed with another extruded component to produce an easily assembled and attractive floor lamp. This is proof that the use of recyclates does not limit or stifle design. Indeed, it can be argued that the constraint of using only recyclates can generate creativity.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Wojtek Stachowicz,USA</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>310</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>One-offs, small batchproduction</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Recycled HDPE</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>341</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>•Recycled materials</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Q</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>IDRAaward, 1995</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>332</td></tr></table>

![](images/78f7a526d1b0a626f4ccb23d72c3ec551feeb71bf956945ac9a006c337608de0.jpg)

![](images/57e804636efd1262c9b4038d806c8fa20d84fdd5d0f7dafa1fa710de56804754.jpg)

## SailbuoyCanvas

Four coloured filters can be fitted to the polypropylenepaper laminate shade to alter lighting mood. A wooden base is fitted with a lampholder for a 1o-watt compact fluorescent lamp (CFL), ensuring low energy consumption and heat output.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Neil Wilson, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>311</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Lampholder2000plc,UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>319</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Polypropylene, paper,wood</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>347</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Multifunctionaltask ormoodlighting</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>329</td></tr></table>

## Table/floorlights

Precision laser-cut Trupan MDF is combined with paper and steel rods with birch ball feet to create an intriguing form, which exposes all the unfinished raw materials. Economical use of natural coloured materials illustrates the ability of such products to compete with more established lighting designs.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Burges Zbryk, E + ZDesign, USA</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>306,311</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>E+ Z Design, USA</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>306</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>MDF, paper, steel,birchwood</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>295,339</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>- Renewable andrecyclable materials</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>IDRA award, 1997</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>332</td></tr></table>

## The Eye of the Peacock

Plastic bottles are shredded and reconstituted to form a fascinating melange of colour and texture, the original bottle tops and sealing rings further enhancing the texture and variety ofthis wall panel. Illuminated from behind with fluorescent lighting. this wall panel illustrates the capacity of new materials to create a visual stimulus.

## Tablelamp

This little lamp   
recontextualizes banal,   
everyday objects such as   
cutlery into a new,   
revitalized, sculptural form. Examining the potential   
uses of existing products has its rewards.

batch and high-volume production, these long cylinders of plastic recyclate illuminated with fluorescent lamps create an eclectic range of decorative lights.

## Viva

Borrowing from the Italian tradition of using readymades espoused by Achille Castiglioni and others, the designers took an existing folding umbreila, called Knirps, and converted it into a quirky, amusing, folding light. Many other products await a similar process of reincarnation.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Manuel RibeiroBandeira de Vianna,Brazil, and FranciscoGomez Paz, Argentina,Domus Academy, Italy</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>306</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Prototype</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Reusedumbrella</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>341</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>1</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Reuse ofexistingproduct</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr></table>

## Valvestem candlestick

This product is the culmination of researching local sources of suitable salvaged manufactured components available in sufficient quantities for large-scale production. The original design was driven by the availability of a stock of valve stems from diesel engines. Other components were tested to see if they would fulfil the required functions of holding the candle and collecting wax. Valve lifter springs proved particularly effective as candleholders and are easily cleaned of excess wax by bending the spring.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>R+r Sustainable Design,USA</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>309</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>R+r Sustainable Design,USA</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>309</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Reused valve stems andliftersprings</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>341</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>.Reuse ofredundantmanufacturedcomponents</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>IDRAaward, 1997</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>332</td></tr></table>

![](images/db2e1e85d0562c5deeb8fa97826e775f297ba712c096e5c064e4bd753b5c42d1.jpg)

## Powerglass®

A sandwich of conductive material, which is completely transparent, is embedded between layers of ordinary glass. Single or multilaminate conductive glass affords different power-carrying capacities, so this patented technology can be used a range of applications for lighting, switches, electronic displays and so on, especially for lowvoltage applications.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>R</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Glas Platz, Germany</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>317</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Glas Platz, Germany</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>317</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Transparentconductivematerial, glass</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>295</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>2</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Transparent electricalconductor</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>329</td></tr></table>

## Moonlight MFL

A robust, weatherproof, semi-translucent, polyethylene material is moulded in four sizes and fitted with different sockets to enable the low-wattage lamps (5-23 watts) to be fixed into the earth or used on hard surfaces. Feel

mood swing coming on? Simply change the coloured bulb filter, choosing from up to 250 colours. Moonlight MFL is a versatile, low-energy, 'mood and colour', indoor/outdoor lighting system.

## Outdoorlight

Plastic recyclate feedstock was worked by rotational moulding to create a tough, translucent, granitelike textured shade. Special fittings were developed to take advantage of the energy savings of compact fluorescent light bulbs.

## Pod Lens

<table><tr><td></td><td>Ross Lovegrove, UK</td><td>308</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>Luceplan, Italy</td><td>320</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>Polycarbonates, electricalcomponents</td><td>296.7</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>Multifunctional lighting system Upgradability and repairability</td><td>329</td></tr></table>

Most lighting is static, irredeemably rooted to the electric cabling built into the fabric of the building. Pod Lens is a modular system of a polycarbonate pod unit with bulb and flex and a series of bases for standard or floor lighting. For indoor or outdoor use, the pods provide for flexible and decorative lighting at the whim of the user.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>全</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Meta Morf, Inc., USA</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>320</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>a</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Meta Morf, Inc., USA</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>320</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Recycled plastic,aluminium scrap, regardsteel</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>347</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Recycled materialsLow-energy lightingsystem</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327,329</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>a</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>IDRA award, 1997</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>332</td></tr></table>

![](images/3aa098b9c839a89d290f33267b20f72e818b5c0bc7d1d4ea8e253db0e9b71605.jpg)

## Solar Bud

A photovoltaic panel generates energy from sunlight, stores it in a battery and releases it to three low-voltage, red LEDs, all in a selfcontained unit, which is placed in the desired position by pushing it into the soil or other suitable medium. Ideal for garden decorative or safety lighting, the Solar Bud would also be at home in the window box of an urban bedsitter.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ross Lovegrove, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>308</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Luceplan, Italy</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>320</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Metals, photovoltaicsand light emitting diodes(LEDs)</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>295</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Solar-poweredlightingVerylow-energy LEDbulbs</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>329</td></tr></table>

## Tsola

Most outdoor, solarpowered lights are aboveground installations, which makes them vulnerable to the elements, accidental damage and vandalism. Tsola is designed to be installed flush with the ground and can be walked or driven upon without damage. This lowmaintenance light is equipped with a timer that automatically switches the light off in extended hours of darkness to conserve the stored energy in the battery.

## Aladdin Power

Generating electricity by using a hand-wound generator is not a new idea. The Russian army has supplied its conscripts with a robust, hand-powered torch since the 1940s and plastic-bodied, handcranked torches have been available since the 1970s. But Nisso Engineering's design uses lighter, modern materials to improve the efficiency of the design and possibly make this an attractive option for powering other hand-held electronics such as mobile phones. However, these handcranked torches do not permit any energy storage in a battery or in a wind-up mechanism as featured in Freeplay Energy's products.

Applications with LED lighting technology encourage economical use of materials, none more so than this credit card-sized torch made of stainless steel in a recyclable casing.

![](images/d3041d60b81a32dcfddbb9115757682cb04816933e7e0e513d151a1e80207433.jpg)

![](images/7d3ff02bdaea9d0d29796ef7026aee757a5fc9943a0b627641eec8b1c08139d9.jpg)

## Freeplay flashlight

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Freeplay Energy Europe,UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>317</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Freeplay Energy Europe,UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>317</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Plastics, rechargeablebattery, electroniccomponents</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>296-7</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>·Human-poweredlightAvoidance ofhazardousconsumables</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>329</td></tr></table>

Manufacturers of the renowned Freeplay wind-up radios have extended their product range with this hand-powered torch. The design represents a significant improvement on the inexpensive but fairly useless small torches produced since the 197os, in which squeezing a lever by hand generates a feeble and rather wobbly light. Now a few minutes' cranking produces a steady, reliable light source without the need to consume lots of toxic batteries.

![](images/4455e4c7b9f4143260162a68d639ca57a02266bc0aa8e61e3c1b4d0bafd39265.jpg)

![](images/f308464e6bc6005ce4c522b727b03cbec72446963d00a46c92cace199c213650.jpg)

## L48 Solarlantern

A photovoltaic panel charges the battery, which can provide up to four hours' light from one charge. The lamp is robust and portable and operates in extreme temperatures.

## SL-Torch

An 8o per cent reduction in materials usage is achieved by making the battery into the handle in this neat torch design. Insert the battery into a housing, which holds the bulb, and twist to turn on the torch.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Antoine Cahen, LesAteliers du Nord,Switzerland</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>305</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/7135fcc29b413ca20b0f8a1330f1fdd0315b9a3da99753fcbef63d5eca0db4ba.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Leclanché, Switzerland</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>319</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/70c971d6f9e9602570c0d78bebad377620735dad1a3a511ff9df5dd18ccd7464.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Battery, bulb, plastic</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>347</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Reductioninmaterialsusage</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>328</td></tr></table>

## Solarislantern

Two hours of sun provide one hour of light for this lantern, which is capable of functioning at -30°℃ (-20°F) and altitudes in excess of 7,000 metres (23,000 feet). Fully charged, the NiMH battery, which is free of mercury, cadmium and lead, will provide light for six hours, but if the battery discharges 90 per cent of its capacity a low-voltage disconnect is automatically triggered. This saves battery life and ensures it will last for up to a thousand recharges.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Light Corporation, USA</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>319</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Light Corporation, USA</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>319</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/23f87b78c93345562f1ed319588b534f47cd350d6f3952836d967aff2349a183.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Photovoltaics, plastics,NiMHbattery</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>341</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>.Renewablepowersource·Avoidance ofhazardous substancesin the battery</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>329</td></tr></table>

![](images/c2961dbc2cd9de86a4886b2f93de8ae7909e3ff275606a96e7639c64ab6dd9de.jpg)

![](images/bae70b956c4b29cefa43b3873491352fa827d5db312c2db51dddabfa43c67b9c.jpg)

## Bulbs and Accessories

## Ecotone Ambiance Slimline

As the process of shrinking the compact fluorescent lamp (CFL) continues, Philips have developed a matt-glass, candle-shaped bulb with an E-14 thread. This encourages use of CFLs in a wider range of light fittings and so can help save energy. Available in 6W, 9W and 11W, the lamps are manufactured with a minimal amount of mercury and lead and have a projected lifespan of six years or so.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Philips Lighting BV,Netherlands</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>309</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Philips Lighting BV,Netherlands</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>309</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Glass, metals includingmercury and lead</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>295</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>·Encouragingenergyconservation</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>329</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>o</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>iF Ecology DesignAward, 2000</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>332</td></tr></table>

## Mini-LynxAmbience

Although this CFL looks like a conventional incandescent bulb and is of the same size, it uses 80 per cent less energy. Making CFLs this size has been a considerable challenge, since the electronic ballast and convoluted fluorescent tubes produce bulbs that often protrude beyond conventional light fittings. Sylvania have extended the market reach by shrinking a standard CFL into the recognizable shape of an incandescent bulb and offering the bulb in white and, unusually, apricot and rose.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Sylvania Design Team,UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>310</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>SLILighting, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>324</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Glass, electronic ballast,plastic</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>295</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Low energyconsumption</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>329</td></tr></table>

![](images/c6eab6faab3a3ae2029a73bd12b9d9cb805dbccdec91812016e251a5bea0b38e.jpg)

## Lampholder 2000

Compact fluorescent lamps (CFLs) with standard bayonet fixings include their own electronic ballast to 'kick-start' the light. Lampholder 200o is a light fitting (in pendant, battenholder, flush-mounted or down-light formats) with an integral electronic ballast suitable for direct usage of four-pinconnector CFLs. After three changes of a four-pin CFL it is more cost-effective than the standard bayonet CFL and reduces materials usage in production.

![](images/288e6d25f521f0d383e5c505c70eed27288970bc6fd82dc178b27a70230e640a.jpg)

## Philips Ecotone Ambiance

Although compact fluorescent lamps consume up to 8o per cent less energy than incandescent tungsten bulbs, the early CFL designs included a Ushaped tube, which often protruded when used in conventional fittings. Philips have created a satisfying compromise by reshaping the CFL to conventional lines and size, while still accommodating the electronic ballast in the base. Bulbs to 5W, 9W or 11W output are to the same basic design. The only

downside of this energyefficient story is that small amounts of mercury are required to manufacture each lamp.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Fons Baohm and Patrickvan de Voorde, PhilipsLighting BV,Netherlands</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>309,311</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/a4d2a117a29c54fcf6c748db4ab4648ca4c6af619e8054fc206696f033f4400c.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Philips Lighting BV,Netherlands</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>309</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/818b0f64bf6b7a5300f70a8ecd8bc96765c87ad1057b5390062a2633359e9f55.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Glass, plastics,electronics, mercury</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>283,295</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>·LowenergyconsumptionMore universal shapefor standard fittingsLonglife</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>329</td></tr></table>

## LED® DecorLED

Light emitting diodes (LEDs) operate on low voltages and are very efficient. Ledtronics offers a range of standard Edison base-fitting AC light bulbs in pear, globular and spot shapes. A bulb fitted with 17 LEDs provides fullspectrum white light equivalent to the illumination provided by a 25-watt conventional tungsten bulb but consumes only 1.7 watts. Typically these LED bulbs generate very little heat, 3.4BTUs/hr, compared with 85BTUs/hr for an equivalent tungsten bulb. Aside from offering huge energy savings, LEDs last up to ten times longer than CFLs and 133 times longer than tungsten bulbs.

![](images/07d7a0192d92d62606689cc164250f6920d2e426eb7aabac7bbbb15a229a8164.jpg)

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ledtronics, USA</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>319</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ledtronics, USA</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>319</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>LEDs, metal, glass</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>295</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Reductioninenergyconsumption·Extendedlifespan</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>329</td></tr></table>

## Food Preparation

## iChef

Induction technology uses magnetic fields to transfer heat from a wire coil, generated by electrical energy, into the contents of a cooking vessel. Almost 95 per cent of the electrical energy is converted into heat, which is claimed to be five times more

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Induced Energy, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>318</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Induced Energy, UK</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>318</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>M</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>etal, glass</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>295</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Ω</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Improvedenergyefficiencyand safety</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>329</td></tr></table>

## Aga

Aga-Rayburn cookers are a symbol of durable, classic design with over half a million units supplied to the UK and exported worldwide. 'Agas', as they are fondly known, have been made near the ironmaking town of Telford, Shropshire, since the beginning of the Industrial Revolution. Indeed, the Coalbrookdale foundry, which supplies castings for the cooker, made sections of the world's first iron bridge in 1779 at Ironbridge. Scrap and pig iron are the raw ingredients to create the handmade cast iron sections that form the basic components of the cooker. Poor-quality castings are simply recycled. Originally a solid-fuel cooker, the Aga-Rayburn has been improved over the years

efficient than a gas ring and significantly faster than a conventional electric hotplate. Glass is used to separate the wire coil from the cooking pan or kettle but, as all the heat is transferred to the vessel, the glass top remains cool, providing a safety advantage over conventional cookers. Precise temperatures can be selected by using a digital display.

to accept oil, natural or propane gas or off-peak electricity, and since the 194os also can provide domestic hot water and central heating needs. Agas are coveted status symbol in any domestic kitchen, representing 'good middleclass taste'. There may be more energy-efficient designs on the market but few can match the lifespan of an Aga, which, of course, can be refurbished and/or recast in the future.

![](images/c944dee7cef9fdafda92099d3e7d3a7e3e228556678b12131d1c7f2f29331775.jpg)

## Solar cooker

Cardboard coated with a special reflective surface focuses the sun's energy on to a dark-coloured cooking pot. In subtropical and tropical regions it is possible to save the equivalent of 30 per cent of the annual firewood consumption of a typical household using this cooker. This device provides people in the

developing world who face fuel wood shortages with the means to sterilize water and have hot food.

## Café Duo HD 1740/42, Cucina Duo

This slimline one- or twocup coffee maker combines aesthetic flair with Philips's high eco-standards. Inline drip filters are easily removed from the top, the product in stand-by mode does not consume any electricity and all parts are marked for recycling.

## Food Preparation

## Glass kettle, glass toaster, microclimate

Stefano Mazzano, director of design at Philips Electronics, coordinated a project whose task was to consider the issue of 'anonymity in design'. In the ultra-competitive marketplace for electronic products, often it is only the brand name that identifies the product, although companies such as Apple Computers have consistently demonstrated that eyecatching aesthetics with product performance will always capture market attention. Consumer surveys conducted by Philips indicated that functionality is not the only criterion on which purchases are made.

![](images/d49ab72498f881b42c823589a4da0c5fe148466a2216145f2f8ab66f7b500a4f.jpg)

Products are purchased because they reflect something of the consumer's personality. This fact was well established in the mid-1990s and is catered for by the Philips-Alessi range of products dating from that time on. The challenge is still to reengage consumers and encourage them to re-

![](images/47e8ed8119672b870c8993d20b9b8211ab172724d29538ea08964e28b49f421f.jpg)

examine kitchen appliances. Philips see a vital combination of enhanced functionality, greater application of digital technology and inclusion of more 'personality' into future appliances. The Glass Kettle, Glass Toaster and Microclimate are conceptual prototypes that strive to reflect this

![](images/9bb7846ee5474b4eb93bc7685b49ad01a2726121d0409f8c9b38bf463ab1b470.jpg)  
new approach. The challenge is still to reengage consumers and encourage them to reexamine kitchen appliances.

![](images/cbccefcd8c43619d61b263af2f14a79b05382cc74169b99c87e31f65a0499101.jpg)

Potential increase in longevity</td><td>329</td></tr></table>

## POLTI Ecologico AS810

For those who suffer from asthma this vacuum cleaner is a boon. Its waterbased filtration system removes 99.99 per cent of dust up to o.3 microns including pollen, dust-mite faeces and cigarette smoke. Paper bags are replaced with a removable water filter, which is emptied after use and has an approximate lifetime of six months.

## Cleaning

## Dyson Dual Cyclone range

Hoover and other major manufacturers of vacuum cleaners watched their global market share decrease as James Dyson's bagless version, using two ultra-fast spinning centrifugal chambers, grabbed a significant market share in the 1990s. Unlike conventional cleaners, in which suction decreases as filters and bags become full, the cyclone system maintains 100 per cent suction and the troublesome task of removing a full bag is replaced with emptying the contents of the main chamber. The original Dyson Dual Cyclone was introduced to the UK in 1986 but didn't find commercial success until 1993, when the DCo2 became the best-selling upright cleaner. Dyson Appliances now produce a range of upright and horizontal vacuum cleaners and the cyclone system has been established worldwide as other manufacturers introduced similar

![](images/e7eb2d790ef8d770289c3ed494924abf608ac30534824ad6baa0893292ce79da.jpg)

![](images/7bd6c7503fb3740eb9af1b594c9d382d9b3499606be8b3ae429e5e55394e1bde.jpg)

machines to their existing ranges. Today the principle of centrifugal vacuum design is well established, eliminating the wastage of vast quantities of paper bags.

<table><tr><td></td><td>James Dyson and Dyson Appliances, UK</td><td>306</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>Dyson Appliances, UK</td><td>315</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>Plastics, motor, electrics</td><td>283, 295</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>Avoids use of consumables (paper bags) • Improved efficiency with greatercleanliness and health Designforeasy maintenance</td><td>327</td></tr></table>

## POLTI Vaporetto 2400

Super-heated steam is an effective agent in cleaning and sterilizing carpets, mattresses and upholstery, which obviates the need to use strong and/or toxic chemical cleaners. Steam is also a safer method than insecticides of killing dust mites and other insects. Tap water is heated in a pressurized steel container and dispersed via the outlet hose, which can be fitted with  range of brushes and hose-ends.

![](images/6d9b707e1127bbf7f33ee10d85983237efae104ae5c49e3c3bddeb20ae5f0bfb.jpg)

with Fuzzy-Logic   
microprocessors in an

## Titan

Ever thought of putting your laundry basket inside your washing machine? This is the solution developed by Monotub Industries, which designed a laundry tub that is inserted at an angle into the machine. Water is delivered from stainlesssteel jets embedded into the walls of the tub, whose load capacity is 40 per cent higher than average machines. With its wideopening door and pushbutton control panel using familiar symbols and icons, the Titan meets EU'A'ratings for wash performance and energy efficiency.

## Cleaning

## Hoover Quattro Whisper Easy Logic model AE230

In 1993 Hoover became the first manufacturer of washing machines to be awarded the EU Eco-label, which sets threshold values of 0.23kWh/kg for energy usage and 15 litres of water per kilogram (1.5gals/lb) of laundry, with its New Wave range of machines. The company has steadily built on its reputation for efficient machines 'A' rated under the EU Energy Label. The current range includes the Quattro Whisper Easy Logic system, which uses sound-absorbing panels and improved suspension to reduce operational noise. A Powerjet spray

system and larger than average drum facilitate wash movement, ensuring that a 5kg (11lb) load takes only 49 litres (10.8gals) of water.

## Staber System 2000

Bucking the trend for horizontal-axis-driven frontloading washing machines, the Staber System 2000 offers a top-loaded machine into which the stainless-steel basket of laundry is introduced. Energy-saving features include the use of a variable-speed motor. Easy access to the internal components can be gained by lifting the front panel and fitting a self-cleaning filter, thus facilitating

maintenance. The manufacturers claim reduced energy, water and detergent consumption.

## Cooling

## OZ23

1.3 Appliances

Curvy lines and a 'rollerball' foot to the door give better access than conventional fridge-freezers to the cold storage and freezer spaces, which here have a capacity of 211 litres (46 gallons). Attention to detail is evident in the use of 'CFCfree' isobutane R6ooA coolant and cyclopentane insulation, which reduce the impact of harmful aerial emissions; in the EU Energy Label 'A'rating for appliances; and in the low noise level of the compressor motor, which hums along at a quiet 38dbA. Whether this model has greater longevity than other current models and is easy to maintain and

![](images/05dec8913070bd35ff0cf5e5bed9daaf393901a25f57236cf2d5e4942f684c57.jpg)

![](images/0280f2e14c36f1468e11b8a09856d8f1971ae959b8506ddf2d663fe63cc63b46.jpg)

repair remain to be seen but an appliance with such personality might just receive a bit more care and attention from the owner. The end-of-life scenario is also important - will Electrolux Zanussi be taking these back?

This DC refrigerator/ freezer of o.33cu m (11.6cu ft) capacity, operates from 12V or 24V and can be sustained with any small, domestic, renewableenergy system using deepcycle batteries, for example, a photovoltaic module capable of generating 150W.

## Cooling

## Supercool™

Traditional refrigerant manufacturing involves the use of chlorofluorocarbons (CFCs, HCFCs) as coolants but Supercool AB have exploited the Peltier effect of a doped bismuth telluride thermocouple, which avoids using any of the ozone-depleting gases. A thermoelectric panel operated on a low-voltage

## Supercool™ box

A panel of thermocouples of doped bismuth telluride is capable of pumping heat and provides the cooling for this transportable refrigeration box suitable for commercial or domestic use at 12V or 24V.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=2>Supercool AB, Sweden</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>324</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Supercool AB, Sweden</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>324</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Thermoelectricmodule,bismuth telluride, plastic</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>341</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Reductioninenergyconsumption·Non-toxic refrigerationsystem free of CFCs orHCFCs</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>329,340</td></tr></table>

![](images/871665e03b619d7008a18c5bedac34cdcbc82c96b6f3aa4b2ac45ec15e8e1b81.jpg)

system (12V, 24V) consumes modest iow to provide sufficient cooling for a small hotel minibar. A further advantage is that the mechanism operates silently, unlike the familiar hum of conventional coolant systems.

![](images/20ea51b37ded1b39297d62bbf5c608503f75c28553202a788fe3f98895e14b04.jpg)

## Vestfrost BK350

Vestfrost is one of the world's largest manufacturers of refrigerators and freezers and took an early lead in showing environmental responsibility by removing all CFCs and HFCs from its model range in 1993.

Using the alterrnative 'Greenfreeze' refrigerants, Vestfrost remains the only manufacturer in Europe holding the EU Eco-label for this category of appliances.

## Sycamore fan

Inspiration for the form of this mono-blade fan came from the humble winged seeds of the sycamore tree This organic design uses recycled PET to create a lightweight blade that is energy efficient and quiet.

## Wind

In the Industrial Revolution iron and steel usurped natural materials, so it is refreshing to see the process cleverly reversed in the housing of this electric fan, in which woven rattan replaces the conventional pressed sheet steel or plastic. At the same time the fan is transformed

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Danny Gasser, MichaelHort, Ben Sheperd andOuisinh Tran, Australia</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>306</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Limited batchproduction</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Recycled PET</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>341</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/84bf717648f17688a62b2550dbd555fd76cc73b911c28bc07b94becf8fb85667.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>•RecycledmaterialsReductioninenergyconsumption</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327,329</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1><img src="images/660d6fb8c0223f2a8e38525c67df5c9549888f0a8e6582ba4e888c3afe959070.jpg"/></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>IDRA award, 1997</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>332</td></tr></table>

## Softfan

Resembling a wilted flower, the stationary fan comes to life as the electric motor builds up speed and centrifugal forces unfurl the fabric 'blades'. Once fully taut, the blades produce a whorl of colour and generate a wide-angle breeze. Unlike conventional metal- or plastic-bladed fans, the Soft Fan requires no guard.

## TENCEL® fashion garments

Three internationally   
renowned designers   
reveal the versatility of a   
manmade fibre using   
natural cellulose derived   
from managed forests.   
TENCEL® is from renewable resources and is   
manufactured in a closedloop clean production   
process. With good   
drapability andwide   
choice of surface finishes and weaves, TENCEL® fabrics offer the convenience and feel of modern synthetics and have a reduced impact on the environment. All   
this is proof that today's   
levels of comfort and   
style can be maintained   
without sacrificing the   
environment. TENCEL® is   
one of the modern success stories of the global textile industry.

## Clothing

## 10939 wedding dress

Hess Naturtextilien examined how the ecoefficiency of a typical wedding dress could be improved, bearing in mind the extravagance of using such a dress for a single day only. Their solution is to make a dress using 40 per cent woven hemp with 6o per cent silk with an organic cotton undergarment and lace. Manufacturing processes dramatically reduce chemical treatments and water consumption. For the first year of its life the dress is lent to a series of happy brides, thereafter it is made available for purchase. Hydrocarbon

dry-cleaning replaces the traditional cocktail of volatile chemicals in between loans. And with luck the dress will be further reused by the bride's friends and relatives before being recycled via textile reclaimers.

<table><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Hess NaturtextilienGmbH, Germany</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>318</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>a</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Hess NaturtextilienGmbH, Germany</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>318</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Hemp, silk, cotton</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>291</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Hire ratherthanownership- Renewable materialsClean production</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>327,328</td></tr><tr><td rowspan=1 colspan=1></td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>Design Sense awards,Shortlist, 1999</td><td rowspan=1 colspan=1>332</td></tr></table>

## Levis Engineered Jeans

Jeans are firmly rooted in popular culture and have been the workwear garment of choice for millions over the last century. Cotton fibre has been the favoured raw material for all jeans manufacturers but cotton fabrics carry a significant raft of environmental burdens from pesticide applications and other toxic agents used in the textile production process. Today Levi Strauss, one of the world's largest clothing manufacturers, uses a special natural cellulosebased, low-environmentalimpact fibre called TENCEL® for its new line of Levis Engineered Jeans.

## New Nomads

Out of the Vision for the Future project originated by Philips in 1997 emerged a collection of seventy prototypes including a

![](images/d69646ff885cc22323da32fc58721c110d7a5fbf116c0bb3f7f5b11f444a81b5.jpg)

range of New Nomads wearable clothing and objects for the IT age. Central to the concept of being able to wander around like a nomad while remaining connected to the information superhighway was the need to recharge equipment. Hence a multipocketed waistcoat becomes a recharging station for handheld electronic equipment, as an attached solar panel stores energy in slim, lightweight batteries.
